# тЦ╢тЦ╢ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE тАФ **Glossary plan-and-execute (Phase 1) SHIPPED** ┬╖ branch `feat/composition-service` ┬╖ HEAD `658a0caa` ┬╖ 2026-06-25

> **WHAT SHIPPED:** the glossary assistant can now do **multi-step ontology goals in ONE confirm** тАФ the agent calls `glossary_plan(book_id, goal)`, a capable model emits a **typed plan**, and a **deterministic executor** applies it (no ReAct loop). Validated against the literature (typed-plan тЙл ReAct; control-flow-integrity against prompt injection). Built as **reusable SDK kits**, not ad-hoc тАФ see [[project_agentic_sdk_lives_in_loreweave_mcp_kit]]: this project will run many agents, so the pattern lives in `sdks/`.
>
> **Specs/plan:** architecture [2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md) ┬╖ kit mechanism [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md](../specs/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md) (Part I envelope/executor/error-classes, Part II concrete Go types + glossary op-set + caps + resolved-questions ledger) ┬╖ multi-agent execution plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md).
>
> **The two new SDK kits (`sdks/go/`):**
> - **`loreweave_mcp`** gained the **plan-action kit** тАФ `plan.go` (frozen K0 contract: `Plan`/`Op`/`OpOutcome`/`Summary`, sentinel errors, `OpSpec`/`Registry`, `NewRegistry` panics on non-idempotent ops, `ValidatePlan` does dedupe + duplicate-conflict + frozen op-N ids + `MaxPlanOps=50` cap + reject-empty) and `execute.go` (`Execute(ctx,userID,plan,enabledOps,reg) Summary` тАФ tier-stable-sort, per-op `errors.Is`тЖТoutcome mapping, destructive-skip via `enabledOps`, internal-error abort). Constants: `PlanVersion=1`, `DescriptorExecutePlan="execute_plan"`, `PlanTokenTTL=30m`.
> - **`loreweave_llm` тАФ NEW Go LLM SDK (the missing piece; user-chosen this session).** Sync streaming client over the provider-registry LLM gateway. `client.go` (`Complete()` accumulates SSE token deltas; reasoning kept separate; internal `/internal/llm/stream?user_id=`+X-Internal-Token OR jwt `/v1/llm/stream`+Bearer), `models.go` (`StreamRequest`, `ReasoningEffort` incl. `none` to disable hidden thinking, `Result{Text,Reasoning,Usage,FinishReason}`), `errors.go` (code-matched sentinels), `sse.go` (8MB-buffer scanner). Zero deps.
>
> **Glossary wiring:** `planRegistry()` ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)) registers **4 additive ops** тАФ adopt_genres(t0), create_kinds(t1), add_attributes(t2), edit_attribute(t4); `glossary_plan` MCP tool ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go)) resolves modelтЖТreads ontologyтЖТruns planner (120s + 1 repair round, loose-emitтЖТvalidateтЖТrepair)тЖТmints a confirm card; confirm/execute path ([plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` re-validates then `mcp.Execute`). chat-service `glossary_skill.py` routes multi-step goals to `glossary_plan` (K=2 stop).

> **тЬЕ PHASE 2 SLICE 1 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ destructive ontology deletes + per-op enable toggle.** Plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md). The planner can now emit **`delete_kind` / `delete_genre`** (tier 5, Destructive), and the confirm card renders each destructive op as an **opt-in checkbox (default OFF)** тАФ a bare "approve" never deletes (G1).
> - **Ops** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)): 3 delete OpSpecs reuse the EXISTING soft-deprecate cascade primitives (`cascadeDeleteBookKind/Genre`, `softDeleteBookAttribute`); **deprecation-aware resolvers** split idempotency тАФ never-existedтЖТ`target_gone` (failed), already-deprecatedтЖТ`already_done` (skipped), liveтЖТdelete (applied).
> - **The wire** ([action_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_confirm.go) `decodeConfirmToken` now reads `enabled_ops`; [plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` validates ids (unknownтЖТ422 `bad_enabled_op`) and passes the set to `mcp.Execute` тАФ **replacing the hardcoded `nil`** that made destructive ops impossible). Preview stamps `op_id`+`destructive` per row + cascade-count notes.
> - **FE** ([ConfirmActionCard.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/chat/components/ConfirmActionCard.tsx)): opt-in checkbox per destructive row + "N will be SKIPPED unless enabled" hint; [actionsApi.ts](../../frontend/src/features/chat/actionsApi.ts) sends `enabled_ops` only when тЙе1 checked; i18n ├Ч4.
> - **ЁЯФО /review-impl тАФ 1 MED + 1 LOW-coverage FIXED, 1 regression caught:** **MED** тАФ `ontologyStateSummary` (the planner's ENTIRE view of current state) listed only kind codes тЖТ `delete_genre`/`delete_attribute` referenced codes the planner had never seen (always `target_gone`). Fixed: summary now lists **genres too** (cheap) so `delete_genre` works; **`delete_attribute` dropped from planner vocab** (kept registered+tested тАФ the `edit_attribute` precedent тАФ since per-attribute context would bloat every plan call AND the GUI already deletes attributes). **LOW** тАФ added a real-PG `delete_genre` round-trip (distinct cascade from kind). **Regression** тАФ the wider test net caught `TestPlanRegistry_BuildsWithoutPanic` asserting Phase-1's `len==4`/no-destructive invariant; updated to the Phase-2 shape (7 ops, exactly the 3 deletes destructive).
> - **VERIFY:** glossary `go build`+`vet` clean; kit tests pass; **real-PG round-trips** (throwaway DB `loreweave_glossary_p2smoke`): `delete_kind` default-skip(`not_confirmed`, stays live)тЖТenableтЖТ`applied`(+cascade)тЖТre-runтЖТ`already_done`тЖТbogusтЖТ`target_gone`; `delete_genre` applyтЖТdeprecatedтЖТ`already_done`; existing confirm/preview round-trips **non-regressive** on real PG; FE **18 tests** (15 card + others) green, `tsc` clean. **тП│ NOT yet smoked: the full chat-UI flow** (`D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`).
>
> **тЬЕ PHASE 2 SLICE 2 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ planner orchestrates the merge action over detected duplicates.** Design+plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md). **Why this and not "entity ops":** two scouts established that glossary **entities are UUID-identified and their names are non-unique EAV values** тЖТ a planner can't safely address an existing entity by name, AND merge/research already exist as class-C confirm actions (`descMerge`/`descDeepResearch`). The user chose the **"planner orchestrates existing actions"** path: the planner *selects* from detected `merge_candidates` (which already carry UUIDs) rather than inventing references.
> - **The op** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go) `merge_candidate`, tier 5 Destructive): params `{candidate_id, winner_id?}` тАФ the planner copies a **stable `candidate_id`** (one PK per cluster) from context, never a transcribed member-UUID or ambiguous name. Handler resolves the candidate's CURRENT members at execute time (no staleness), picks winner (`winner_id` if a member тЖТ else detector's `suggested_winner` тЖТ else `ErrBadParams`), and calls the EXISTING `mergeEntitiesCore` (journals + marks-merged). One op **per candidate** тЖТ each gets its own slice-1 enable toggle.
> - **Idempotency made independent of the best-effort `markCandidatesMerged` flip** (a test-driven fix тАФ it lags under load): statusтЙа`proposed`тЖТ`already_done` fast-path AND **0 losers actually merged тЖТ `already_done`**, so a re-run is deterministic. Bogus idтЖТ`target_gone`.
> - **Planner context** ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go) `mergeCandidatesSummary`, cap 25 by score, **best-effort** тАФ additive, never fails an ontology plan): each cluster's `candidate_id` + member names/ids/link-counts + suggested winner + rationale. Vocab + destructive guidance updated. Preview resolves candidateтЖТwinner-kept/losers-merged names.
> - **ЁЯФО /review-impl тАФ 1 MED (prompt-injection) FIXED + tested:** the candidate context block embeds untrusted **entity names + detector rationale** (extraction/user content) into the planner's **SYSTEM** prompt тАФ the first free-text there (kind/genre codes are slugs). The user-supplied `reference` is fenced as DATA in the user message, but the candidate block was not. Fixed: `safePromptField` runs `sanitize.NeutralizeCanonText` (the canon-boundary defense the deep-research path already uses тАФ inert-izes chat-template/role markers + invisibles) + collapses whitespace (a smuggled newline can't forge a block field) + caps length. Regression tests assert a `<|im_start|>` / "ignore previous instructions" / newline-injected name is neutralized. (Blast radius was already bounded by the enable-toggle gate тАФ every destructive op is default-OFF + shown тАФ but untrusted text in the system prompt is a defense-in-depth gap worth closing.)
> - **VERIFY:** build/vet clean; kit green; **real-PG round-trip**: `merge_candidate` enableтЖТ`applied` (loser soft-deleted via `mergeEntitiesCore`)тЖТre-runтЖТ`already_done`тЖТbogusтЖТ`target_gone`; +DB-free validate/parse/dedupe + `resolveMergeWinner` + neutralization unit; registry guard now **8 ops** (4 destructive). FE **unchanged** тАФ the slice-1 toggle already renders any destructive plan op (merge_candidate rows carry `op_id`+`destructive`). **тП│ chat-UI flow still `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`** (now also covers the merge path).

The kit's sentinel set has no "rejected" reason; not worth a new one.
> **тЬЕ SLICE 2.5 SHIPPED тАФ `dismiss_candidate` (non-destructive sibling of merge).** Same candidate-id addressing; the planner emits `dismiss_candidate {candidate_id}` to REJECT a detected cluster as not-duplicates (flips statusтЖТ`dismissed`, no entity touched), reusing the existing `dismissMergeCandidateCore`. **NON-destructive** тЖТ applies on a plain confirm with NO enable toggle (the real-PG test asserts exactly this via `f.confirm` without `enabled_ops`). Idempotency: statusтЙа`proposed`тЖТ`already_done`; bogusтЖТ`target_gone`. Registry guard now **9 ops** (5 non-destructive incl. dismiss, 4 destructive). **ЁЯФС also fixed a latent test-isolation bug:** the candidate real-PG tests were silently depending on another test creating `merge_candidates` first тАФ now they run the full idempotent `runMergeMigrations` chain (correct post-G4 `kind_id`тЖТ`book_kinds` FK), so they pass in isolation.

VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestResearchJob_CRUD_RealPG` (kind-validate, live count ignores soft-deleted, insertтЖТload round-trip, list, one-live unique constraint, slot-frees-on-complete). **M2:** in-process worker draining `pending` jobs (reuse glossary's `internal/events/*` background pattern), skip-already-researched idempotency, `cursor_entity_id` crash-resume, pause/resume/cancel endpoints, cost-metering, live web-search smoke. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE + launch action on the kind + polling + i18n ├Ч4. Full design (with superseding M1 note): [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` (original design-checkpoint note)~~ тАФ **DESIGNED 2026-06-25 (gate 2: large/structural, XL cross-cutting).** The research-orchestration item was scoped: wiring it as a **synchronous plan op** is the wrong vehicle (the `execute_plan` executor runs in-request тЖТ a paid N-entity web-search fan-out would be minutes-long, timeout-prone, no progress/cancel; AND deep-research's "agent summarizes the attached evidence" design has no agent in the deterministic executor). **Decision (user, 2026-06-25): build it as an async batch-research JOB** hosted in **glossary-service** (it owns entities/evidence/the BYOK web-search; glossary already runs in-process `internal/events/*` consumers тАФ no need to proxy to knowledge like wiki does). Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md) тАФ `entity_research_jobs` table + one-live-per-(book,kind) lock, a knowledge-modeled state machine (pendingтЖТrunningтЖТpaused_budget/paused_userтЖТcomplete/failed/cancelled) with a per-search `max_spend` ceiling + cursor crash-resume, a `researchOneEntity` refactor that shares INV-6 web-search/neutralize/attach with the existing sync `glossary_deep_research`, 6 owner/grant-gated endpoints + pre-flight cost estimate, and a `ResearchJobCard` FE. Phased M1 (core+schema+endpoints) тЖТ M2 (worker+lifecycle) тЖТ M3 (FE). Entity create/edit/rename remain **out** (UUID-addressed, names ambiguous тАФ GUI/EAV only).

> # тЦ╢тЦ╢ (prior epic) **Multilingual KG epic COMPLETE (M1тАУM7)** ┬╖ branch `feat/kg-multilingual` ┬╖ 2026-06-24 тАФ ready to PR

> **EPIC: canonical-source + derived-localization multilingual KG.** Design fully clarified + red-teamed + gate-validated. Spec [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md) ┬╖ detailed design [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md) ┬╖ plan (7 milestones) [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md). Design commit `1a448661`.
>
> **Core decisions (locked):** Layer-1 graph stays source-language, NEVER re-extracted from translations; derive a localization layer (L2 labels) + language-aware retrieval (L3) on top. vi content is **index-only + label-only**. **Gates:** V1 PASS (bge-m3 genuinely multilingual тЖТ single-vector-space premise holds), V7 PASS-caveat (reranker BYOK-optional тЖТ D5 must work WITHOUT it), V6 FAIL (lexical leg not CJK-tokenized тЖТ D12 real). D5 uses **soft language-preference weighting** (not hard filter тАФ robust to partial translation + dodges Neo4j post-filter limit).
>
> **тЬЕ M1 FOUNDATION SHIPPED (this session) тАФ `source_lang` tag + cost metering + skip-gate.** All additive + dormant (source_lang unread until M4); no behavior change to existing retrieval.
> - **`source_lang`/`mixed`/`content_hash` on `:Passage`** (Neo4j-only тАФ DD1: passages have NO Postgres mirror) тАФ model + Cypher ON CREATE/MATCH + `upsert_passage` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)). New helpers `set_source_lang_for_source` (tag-only backfill) + `get_source_ingest_state` (skip-gate read: hash+canon+chapter_index).
> - **Ingester** ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)): `resolve_source_lang` (declared chapter `original_language` wins, else `detect_primary_language`, "mixed"тЖТflag); **content-hash skip-gate** (skip embed+re-bill ONLY when text+canon+chapter_index all match тАФ a draftтЖТpublish canon flip or reorder still re-ingests); **cost metering** via existing `record_spending` (DD2 тАФ fixes the pre-existing embed-token leak; `pool` threaded through the publish handler + backfill); `backfill_source_lang` (tag-only, per-chapter declared lang). `backfill_published_passages` now passes declared `source_lang` + `pool`; both wired at PUT embedding-model ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)).
> - **book-service** already returns `original_language` in `/internal/books/{id}/chapters` (server.go:2244) тЖТ no Go change; `BookClient.list_chapters` surfaces it verbatim.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3017 passed** (source_lang/cost-metering/skip-gate tests + regression tests for canon-flip, chapter reorder, model-change, regional-subtag). **LIVE-SMOKE deferred тЖТ folds into M2's mandatory cross-service smoke** (M2 dual-indexes vi passages through this same ingest path + asserts source_lang) тАФ `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **/review-impl (cold-start adversary) тАФ 1 HIGH + 2 MED FIXED, 2 LOW accepted:** **HIGH** тАФ the skip-gate ignored `embedding_model`; since the model-set path's `_delete_project_graph` deletes only graph labels (`Entity/Event/Fact/ExtractionSource`), NOT `:Passage`, a model change would have skip-gated re-embedding тЖТ stale-dimension vectors. Fixed: `get_source_ingest_state` returns `embedding_model` + the skip condition compares it (re-ingests on model change). **MED** тАФ draft-index path (`raw_search.py`) didn't meter embed spend тЖТ now passes `pool` (guarded); + `source_lang` now normalized to ISO-639-1 primary subtag (`zh-CN`тЖТ`zh`) so M4 ranking won't drift. **Accepted+documented (LOW):** `get_source_ingest_state` LIMIT-1 is safe (one ingest writes identical hash/canon/index/model to all chunks); `record_spending` best-effort silent-fail is intentional.
>
 **тЬЕ M2 EVENT + DUAL-INDEX SHIPPED (this session).** A chapter's ACTIVE translation is now dual-indexed as `source_lang`-tagged `:Passage` nodes тАФ index-only (NEVER re-extracts Layer 1, R1).
> - **Producer (translation-service):** `translation.published` outbox event (`aggregate_type='translation'` тЖТ `loreweave:events:translation`) emitted at ALL 3 active-version chokepoints (DD5): manual `set_active_version`, human-block-edit `patch_translation_block`, and the auto-promote in `chapter_worker._finalize_chapter` (the one that emitted NOTHING before тАФ only when the promote actually changed the active row). One envelope via [translation_events.py](../../services/translation-service/app/translation_events.py).
> - **vi-text retrieval:** new internal `GET /internal/translation/chapters/{chapter_id}/active-text?target_language=` ([main.py](../../services/translation-service/app/main.py)) returns the active version's flat text (translated_body, else assembled from translated_body_json) тАФ the translated text lives in translation-service, NOT book-service.
> - **Consumer (knowledge-service):** new [translation_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/translation_client.py) + `handle_translation_published` ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py)) resolves project by book_id тЖТ fetches vi text тЖТ `ingest_chapter_passages(text_override=vi, source_lang=vi, canon=True)`. Registered: stream `loreweave:events:translation` ([consumer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/consumer.py)) + dispatcher ([main.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/main.py)).
> - **Language-scoped passage identity (DD1):** `passage_canonical_id` now includes `source_lang` so vi/zh chunks of the SAME chapter (shared `source_id=chapter_id`, kept clean for hitтЖТchapter mapping) are DISTINCT nodes; `delete_passages_for_source` + `get_source_ingest_state` gained a `source_lang` filter so re-ingesting vi never wipes zh (and vice-versa). vi uses `source_type='chapter'` тЖТ the existing retriever finds it (M4 adds language ranking). Empty/'unknown' lang keeps pre-M2 ids byte-identical.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3022 passed** (canonical-id distinctness, text_override bypass, handler dual-index/skip-no-text/skip-no-project); translation-service **137 relevant + 2 emit-envelope** tests pass, py_compile clean.
> - **тЬЕ LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-24, on the running stack) тАФ clears `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.** Target: Dracula book `019ef35c-36c3-тАж` / chapter `019ef35c-3f4c-тАж` (active vi translation, knowledge project `019ef35e-d08f-тАж`, embed model `019e7f71-тАж`). **(1) Endpoint:** `active-text?target_language=vi` тЖТ 5939 chars assembled from `translated_body_json`. **(2) Event path:** inserted `translation.published` outbox row in `loreweave_translation` тЖТ worker-infra relay (`published_at` set) тЖТ `loreweave:events:translation` тЖТ `knowledge-extractor` group тЖТ handler тЖТ **6 vi `:Passage`** created (was 0; the 6 originals are `source_lang=null`). **(3) Data model (DD1):** vi passages carry `source_lang='vi'`, clean `source_id=chapter_id`, `embedding_1024` (dim=1024, V1), `canon=TRUE`, `content_hash` set. **(4) Idempotency:** re-emit (relayed + consumed, lag 0) тЖТ still **6** vi passages, no dupes/re-embed. **тЪа The smoke caught a real defect mock-VERIFY can't:** the deployed knowledge-service image was **stale** (cached Docker `COPY` layer) тЖТ running consumer subscribed to only 3 streams (no `translation`) тЖТ the event would've been silently dropped. Fixed via `--no-cache` rebuild + `--force-recreate`; confirmed the consumer now subscribes to all 4 streams. (Cost-metering `record_spending` is best-effort/non-blocking тАФ lightly covered live; phantom-pricing tracked separately in `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING`.)
>
> **тЬЕ M3 READER-LANGUAGE STORAGE SHIPPED (this session) тАФ book-service only.** Per-(user,book) reader-language preference, server SSOT + cross-device (DD3).
> - **Table** `user_book_prefs(user_id, book_id, reader_language, updated_at, PK(user_id,book_id))` in book-service schema ([migrate.go](../../services/book-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)) тАФ no FK (matches `reading_progress`; existence gated at handler).
> - **Public endpoints** `GET/PUT /v1/books/{book_id}/reader-language` ([reader_language.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/reader_language.go)) тАФ view-gated via `canViewOrPublic` (grant OR public book, so a reader of a shared/public novel keeps a pref; non-grantee private book тЖТ 404, no oracle). PUT validates a BCP-47-ish tag (`langTagRe`, тЙд35 chars); empty/whitespace тЖТ clears (delete тЖТ null). NOT localStorage (CLAUDE.md). Registered NOT in the mutating-grant table (per-user data = view-tier write, like favorites); GET added to `grant_mapping_test` readRoutes.
> - **Internal resolver** `GET /internal/books/{book_id}/reader-language?user_id=` (token-gated, user_id param like `getBookAccess`) тАФ the cross-service source for M4 retrieval + M7 consumers. The FALLBACK chain (reader-pref тЖТ detected query lang тЖТ source lang) lives at the CONSUMER (M4) where query+source langs are known тАФ M3 only exposes the stored value.
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean; `internal/api` green (ReaderLang nil-pool auth/tenancy/validation + grant-mapping). **тЬЕ LIVE-SMOKE on real PG18** (`loreweave_book_m3smoke` throwaway): setтЖТGETтЖТ**AC3 cross-request read**тЖТinternal-resolverтЖТupsertтЖТclearтЖТnull + **per-user isolation** (two users, independent prefs, no shared row) all PASS. Full book-service suite green тАФ also fixed a **pre-existing** stale `config.TestLoadValidation` (didn't set the now-required `CONFIRM_TOKEN_SIGNING_SECRET`; one-line, same-service, fix-now).
>
> **тЬЕ M4 LANGUAGE-AWARE RETRIEVAL SHIPPED (this session) тАФ D5 soft-weighting (cross-service: book + knowledge).** The core user-facing win: a reader's language preference softly re-ranks hybrid search so they get content in their language first.
> - **Both legs carry `sourceLang`:** semantic from the passage prop ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py) `passage_to_hit`); lexical from the chapter's `original_language` тАФ book-service `lexical-search` now selects `c.original_language` in all 3 SQLs + threads it through `buildLexicalHit` ([search.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/search.go)).
> - **Soft language ordering, [hybrid_fusion.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/hybrid_fusion.py) `apply_language_preference`** (revised by /review-impl тАФ see below): a **stable matched-first partition** applied as the FINAL ordering pass (post-rerank, post-floor, pre-cap). Reader-language hits move to the front, preserving the upstream rerank/RRF order within the matched + unmatched groups; nothing is dropped (soft, robust to partial translation). `"mixed"` matches any pref. `normalize_lang` = ISO-639-1 primary subtag (mirrors M1 `resolve_source_lang`). Scale-independent (no weight to tune).
> - **ЁЯФО /review-impl тАФ 2 HIGH FIXED (both masked by the original smoke):** (1) the boost was **inert under rerank** тАФ applied pre-rerank, but `pool_n тЙе len(fused)` always, so the cross-encoder re-sorts the whole list by relevance and discards it тЖТ moved the language step to the FINAL pass + made it scale-independent (the additive `w_lang`/`settings.lang_pref_weight` is removed). (2) the RRF fusion key `_hit_key` **omitted `sourceLang`**, so a chapter's vi + zh/en passages (same `chapterId`+`surface`+`chunkIndex`) collided and one language was dropped BEFORE ranking тЖТ added `sourceLang` to `_hit_key` (mirrors `passage_canonical_id`/DD1). Both have regression tests (rerank-on ordering; both-languages-survive-fusion). Re-smoked live (rerank default-on path): `language=en`тЖТ6 en then 6 vi (**n=12, both survive**); `language=vi` & reader-prefтЖТ6 vi then 6 en.
> - **Resolver chain** ([raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)): `?language=` тЖТ the CALLER's stored reader-language (M3, via new `BookClient.get_reader_language` тЖТ `/internal/books/{id}/reader-language`) тЖТ `detect_primary_language(query)` тЖТ None. It's the SEARCHER's pref (caller), not the resolve-to-owner project user. `run_hybrid_search` gained `pref_lang` (None default тЗТ wiki in-process path unchanged).
> - **VERIFY:** book-service `go build`+`go vet` clean, api tests green; knowledge **98 unit** pass (boost math/sort/mixed/regional, resolution chain, both legs carry sourceLang); provider-gate OK. **тЬЕ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service) on the Dracula stack:** reader-pref `vi` resolved via book-service internal endpoint тЖТ boost applied (`score=fused+0.0164`); with **identical-embedding en/vi clones** (language = sole variable) `language=en`тЖТen ranks #0, `language=vi` & reader-prefтЖТvi ranks #0 тАФ the boost alone flips ranking, M3тЖТM4 chain end-to-end. (Confirmed the M2 dual-index is bug-free: a clean re-index yields exactly 6 vi; an earlier "12 passages" was smoke-data pollution from consumer replay-from-0 + repeated manual emits, now reset.)
>
> **тЬЕ M5 PART 1 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ C5 predicate labels + C4 kind name_i18n.** M5 is larger than the plan's "size M" (4 features across glossary + knowledge); sliced by risk boundary.

Endpoint `GET /v1/knowledge/predicate-labels?language=&codes=` ([labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/labels.py)) тАФ backend-served (R5), `codes` resolves a set else returns the catalog. 13 tests.
> - **C4 kind name_i18n (DD4, glossary-service):** migration `0037_kind_name_i18n` ([kind_name_i18n.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/kind_name_i18n.go)) тАФ `name_i18n JSONB` on `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`/`book_kinds` (inherits each tier's scope, no new tenancy surface) + admin-seeds the 12 System kinds in vi (en `name` stays the en label). The internal ontology reads (`/internal/users/{id}/glossary-standards`, `/internal/books/{id}/ontology`) now surface `name_i18n` via `loadKindNameI18n` (JSONBтЖТ[]byteтЖТunmarshal, graceful nil on un-migrated DB), tier-correct (a user kind shadows by code AND label). **Tenancy:** System is admin-seed-only (never a user write тАФ the `entity_kinds` bug); per-user/book label authoring deferred (`D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS`). Verified on real PG: `character`тЖТ"Nh├вn vс║нt", `location`тЖТ"─Рс╗Лa ─Сiс╗Гm".
>
> **тЬЕ M5 PART 2 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ C9 entity-name vi read + C7 graph-view/timeline label resolution + the SystemтЖТbook label-inheritance fix.** AC1 now works: a vi reader's KG graph-view shows vi kind, entity-name + predicate labels.
> - **C9 (glossary-service):** new internal `POST /internal/books/{book_id}/entity-display-names` ([entity_display_names_handler.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/entity_display_names_handler.go)) тЖТ `{entity_id, display_name, translated}[]`, resolving each entity's `name`/`term` attr to a language via the SAME `attribute_translations` join as the public `display_language` list (reuses the existing translate pipeline's idempotent population тАФ no new write/migration). Internal-token gated, book-scoped, soft-absent for unknown ids. The entity-name vi **population** rides the existing batch-translate (`internalApplyTranslations`, idempotent on `(attr_value_id, language_code)`, never overwrites `verified`); C9 is the READ the KG joins on.
> - **C7 (knowledge-service):** `GraphNode` gains `kind_label`/`name_label`, `GraphEdge` gains `edge_type_label`, `EdgeTimeline`/`TimelineInstance` gain `edge_type_label`/`target_label_localized` (all additive, None тЗТ canonical source-fallback = AC1's "explicit fallback marker"). Pure decorators [graph_labels.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/graph_labels.py) + reader-lang param hygiene [reader_lang.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/reader_lang.py) (shared with M4). `read_graph` + `read_edge_timeline` ([graph_views.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/graph_views.py)) gained `?language=` тЖТ caller's reader-pref fallback; resolve kindтЖТ`name_i18n[lang]` (glossary ontology client, now carrying `name_i18n` + a `kind_labels(lang)` helper), predicateтЖТ`resolve_predicate_label` (C5), entity nameтЖТglossary C9 batch (TRANSLATED-only, so name_label=None is an honest fallback). Best-effort throughout (glossary outage тЖТ canonical graph, never 500). `read_graph` reuses the grant gate's already-resolved `(owner, book_id)` (project_meta_dep) тАФ no extra query.
> - **ЁЯФС Real gap caught by the live-smoke + fixed now (correctness):** for a BOOK-bound KG project, kind labels resolve from `get_book_ontology`тЖТ`book_kinds.name_i18n`, which is EMPTY (C4 seeded only System). Per the LOCKED tier-merge (System defaults тЖТ Per-book, higher shadows by code), a book kind must INHERIT the System vi label unless overridden. `internalBookOntology` (and the user-standards shadow path) now merge `system_kinds.name_i18n тКХ tier.name_i18n` via a new `mergeLabels` ([internal_ontology_read.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/internal_ontology_read.go)). Without this, book-bound graph-views (the common case) showed NO kind labels.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3067 passed** (new: 11 graph_labels pure + 1 graph-view localization router + 3 glossary-client `fetch_entity_display_names` + 5 existing graph-view tests fixed for the new deps); glossary `go build`/`vet` clean + real-PG tests green (C9 4 tests, book-ontology System-inheritance, GlossStandards). provider-gate OK. **тЬЕ AC1 LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (cross-service: knowledgeтЖТglossaryтЖТPGтЖТNeo4j):** seeded a vi name translation + a 2-node `character`/`ALLY_OF` graph on book `019ed9d0тАж`; `GET /v1/kg/projects/{id}/graph?language=vi` тЖТ node `kind_label="Nh├вn vс║нt"` (System label inherited by the book kind тАФ proves the merge fix live), `name_label="A-ri-a"` (C9), edge `edge_type_label="─Сс╗Уng minh cс╗зa"` (C5); untranslated node тЖТ `name_label=null`; **no-language тЖТ fully canonical (back-compat)**. Smoke artifacts cleaned up.
>
> **тЬЕ M6 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ CJK lexical leg via a Neo4j `cjk` full-text index (D12 / V6).** A short Chinese/Japanese/Korean keyword (the pg_trgm-FAIL case) now recalls the right passage.
> - **V6 probe (decisive):** `db.index.fulltext.listAvailableAnalyzers()` тЖТ Neo4j ships a built-in **`cjk`** analyzer (bi-grams); Postgres `pg_available_extensions` тЖТ only `pg_trgm` (NO `zhparser`/`pg_jieba` тЖТ those would need a custom image = infra change). **Decision (per the plan's "pick by probe + infra cost"): Neo4j `cjk`** тАФ zero infra change, and `:Passage` already carries `source_lang` (M1/M2). The book-service Postgres `language`-param/`pg_jieba` route from the plan is therefore moot; book-service trigram stays as the script-agnostic fallback (its `ILIKE` substring still finds exact CJK, just unranked/unindexed).
> - **Schema:** `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX passage_text_cjk_ft тАж OPTIONS {indexConfig:{fulltext.analyzer:'cjk'}}` over `:Passage(text)` ([neo4j_schema.cypher](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_schema.cypher), idempotent, applied on startup).
> - **Repo:** `find_passages_by_fulltext` + `lucene_escape` + `PASSAGE_CJK_FT_INDEX` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)) тАФ global FT index тЖТ oversample + post-filter on `user_id`/`project_id`/`source_lang`/`canon` (mirrors `find_passages_by_vector`); raw query Lucene-escaped (CJK runs kept) so a stray `+`/`:` can't break the parse; blank тЖТ no call.
> - **Retriever:** new `query_has_cjk` (codepoint test тАФ a bare proper noun is too short for language detection) + a `_cjk_lexical` leg ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py)) fused as a THIRD RRF input, gated on `mode!=semantic AND query_has_cjk`. **Additive тАФ empty for Latin queries, so non-CJK retrieval is byte-identical to pre-M6.** Best-effort (FT index missing / query error тЖТ `degraded['cjk_lexical']`, never 500). Needs no embedding model (works on an un-embedded project).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3076 passed** (new: `query_has_cjk`, `lucene_escape`, `find_passages_by_fulltext` wiring + blank-skip, retriever CJK-leg-fuses + Latin-skips; an autouse stub makes the pre-existing CJK-query retriever tests see the leg as additive-empty). **тЬЕ AC LIVE-SMOKE PASSED:** seeded a zh passage (`цЯ│цЧах░Шш╡░ф╕КхдйхЙСх│░я╝МшзБхИ░ф║ЖхзЬхнРчЙЩуАВ`) under the real book owner; `search?query=хдйхЙС&mode=lexical` (**2-char** тАФ the exact pg_trgm-FAIL case) тЖТ returns the block (cjk leg, relevance 2.53); `query=хзЬхнРчЙЩ` (3-char) тЖТ relevance 5.05; `degraded={}`. Smoke artifacts cleaned up. The cjk index was confirmed created on container startup.
> - **Note (cosmetic):** `passage_to_hit` labels every passage hit `matchType:"semantic"` regardless of which passage leg (vector vs cjk-FT) found it тАФ a passage-node-vs-chapter-node distinction, not a correctness issue. Tracked as `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` if it ever matters for UI.
>
> **тЬЕ M7 SHIPPED (this session) тАФ consumer wiring + headline E2E. THE EPIC IS COMPLETE.** Reader-language now threads end-to-end so a vi reader gets vi-first grounding + an honest coverage note.
> - **knowledge (C12):** `drawers/search` + raw-search take `?language=` тЖТ soft matched-first partition (not a filter тАФ off-language hits still surface) + a `coverage` note ({reader_lang,total,in_language,partial,note}) via new `hybrid_fusion.language_coverage`; each drawer hit carries `source_lang` ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py), [raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)).
> - **composition (C6):** `pack()` resolves the author's reader-language ONCE (`BookClient.get_reader_language` тЖТ book-service M3 internal) + threads `language` into the glossary `select_for_context` (per-language aliases) + knowledge `search_drawers` (vi-first lore) lenses, base + delta ([pack.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/pack.py), [lenses.py](../../services/composition-service/app/packer/lenses.py)). Best-effort (None тЖТ source-language).
> - **chat:** already wired (S6 `display_language` тЖТ `build_context`); the FE sends the reader language.
> - **FE (C12):** drawers search sends the UI language; `RawDrawersTab` shows the coverage banner; `DrawerResultCard` badges each hit's `source_lang`; +i18n (en/vi/ja/zh-TW).
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3079** + composition pack/client + FE **20** green; tsc clean; provider-gate OK. **тЬЕ HEADLINE E2E LIVE (тЙе3 services):** (1) drawers `?language=vi` on identical-embedding vi/zh clones тЖТ **vi first** (cosine tied 0.4888 тЗТ ordering is purely the language partition) + `coverage {total:2,in_language:1,partial:true}`; (2) book-service internal reader-language тЖТ `vi`; (3) **composition grounding** `works/{p}/scenes/{n}/grounding` (composition pack тЖТ book reader-lang=vi тЖТ knowledge drawers/glossary `language=vi` тЖТ provider-registry embed) тЖТ `grounding_available=true, token_count=4589, no warnings`. **тЬЕ FE browser smoke (Playwright):** on the built bundle the drawers Raw tab renders the **coverage banner** + a per-hit **language badge** on every hit (`vi├Ч7, zh├Ч1`), driven by the live API. **GOTCHA:** a pre-existing **host vite-dev server** (a node process from a different/stale working copy) shadows :5174 ahead of the docker nginx container тАФ so the built FE was smoked via a temp container on the compose network (`docker run --network infra_default -p 5199`). Also fixed a pre-existing **infra drift**: recreating the frontend cascaded an `api-gateway-bff` restart that crash-looped on a missing `ROLEPLAY_SERVICE_URL` (the BFF `requireEnv`'s it; compose never set it) тЖТ added it to the BFF env in [docker-compose.yml](../../infra/docker-compose.yml).
>
> **ЁЯОЙ EPIC DONE тАФ the driving scenario works:** a vi reader who can't read the zh/en source gets KG content, kind/predicate/entity labels, lexical recall, retrieval ranking, cowrite grounding, and coverage signalling in Vietnamese. **Branch `feat/kg-multilingual` is ready to PR into main.** Remaining tracked rows are all out-of-scope deferrals (see below). **Suggested next:** open the PR, or pick up `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` (formal multilingual eval golden-set) when a corpus exists.
>
> **тЦ╢ Deferred тАФ debt-minimisation pass (2026-06-24): the list was triaged + shrunk; only 3 genuine rows remain, each clearing the defer-gate.**
> - `D-KG-ML-M4-EVAL` тАФ **BLOCKED (gate 4):** formal multilingual eval golden-set needs a curated corpus + judge ensemble (large/structural). The matched-first partition is parameter-free (no weight to sweep); the mechanism is proven by unit tests + the live reordering smoke. Target: when a multilingual eval corpus exists.
> - `D-KG-ML-WIKI` тАФ **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1):** per-language wiki article BODIES is a separate feature (translate long-form wiki text), not part of the KG localization layer.
> - `D-KG-ML-PERUSER-LABELS` тАФ **OUT OF SCOPE (gate 1/5):** per-user kind-label AUTHORING UI/API. System+Book tiers cover the scenario today (a user overrides a label via a book kind; C4 merge gives System defaults). Build only if users ask to author their own per-user kind labels.
> - `D-KG-ML-DRAWERS-METER` тАФ **PRE-EXISTING (not this branch) + product decision (gate 4/5):** whether read-path search embeds should bill the user's BYOK budget. `drawers/search` computes `embedding_cost_usd` but doesn't `record_spending`; this predates kg-multilingual. Deferred pending a product call.
>
> **тЬЕ Recently cleared (this debt pass):**
> - тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-M6-MATCHTYPE` тАФ **FIXED:** `passage_to_hit` gained a `match_type` param; the cjk full-text leg now labels its hits `"lexical"` (was the hardcoded `"semantic"`). +retriever test.
> - тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-MULTI-TRANSLATION-LANG` тАФ **CLEARED:** design proven тАФ `test_canonical_id_distinct_per_language` now asserts zh+vi+en translations of ONE chapter are 4 distinct ids (incl. back-compat). (A live smoke with a real 2nd target language is naturally blocked until a book has one; the coexistence design is proven.)
> - тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-PERLANG-EMBED` тАФ **WON'T-FIX:** the V1 probe PASSED (bge-m3 is genuinely multilingual тЖТ single-vector-space premise holds), so a per-language embedding model is unnecessary. Reopen only if a future target language shows weak recall.
> - тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING` тАФ **VERIFIED MITIGATED:** the phantom-cost concern was "`cost_per_token` gets a model UUID тЖТ fallback rate", but BOTH the ingest ([passage_ingester.py:432](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) and drawers ([drawers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/drawers.py)) pass `embed_result.model` (the RESOLVED provider name); local models map to $0 via the `bge-`/`ollama/`/`lm_studio/` prefixes in [pricing.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/pricing.py). Residual edge (a provider reporting a non-name model string) folds into pricing.py's existing "swap for a dynamic pricing table" note тАФ not this branch's debt.
> - тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + тЬЕ `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE` (live-smoke PASSED 2026-06-24).
>

# тЦ╢тЦ╢ (prior) roleplay-service v1 COMPLETE (R0тЖТR3; cutover done) ┬╖ branch `feat/roleplay-service` ┬╖ HEAD `<pending>` ┬╖ 2026-06-24

> **Building the lean Rust `roleplay-service` (the MMO roleplay-service seed) тАФ R0тЖТR3 continuous XL run.** Spec [2026-06-24-roleplay-scripted-acting.md](../specs/2026-06-24-roleplay-scripted-acting.md), detailed design [2026-06-24-roleplay-service-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-24-roleplay-service-detailed-design.md), plan [2026-06-24-roleplay-service.md](../plans/2026-06-24-roleplay-service.md). KEPT: `/evaluate` (M6 debrief), `POST /internal/chat/sessions` (roleplay's start target), the turn loop + M3 anchoring. `session_templates` table + its seed left in place (harmless, test data). chat-service rebuilt+recreated. **Post-cutover live verify:** `/v1/chat/templates`тЖТ404; `/v1/chat/sessions`тЖТ200; `/v1/roleplay/scripts`тЖТ3 presets; `/start`тЖТ201 (full orchestration intact).

If `/v1/roleplay` 502s after a stack churn, check `docker exec infra-api-gateway-bff-1 sh -c 'echo $ROLEPLAY_SERVICE_URL'` and force-recreate.

The idempotent insert IS the backfill primitive (safe to re-invoke); a re-start is a no-op (integration-tested: one rp_sessions/rp_memory row). When the executive (v2) lands it can backfill-on-read from the chat seed if `rp_memory` is absent тАФ no separate reconcile worker needed.

# тЦ╢ PRIOR тАФ **interview-roleplay POC COMPLETE (M1тЖТM7)** ┬╖ branch `feat/interview-roleplay` ┬╖ HEAD `112c907d` ┬╖ 2026-06-24

> **Anti-drift interview-practice roleplay тАФ the POC for the future roleplay-service тАФ is DONE end-to-end (M1тАУM7).** Spec [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../specs/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md), plan [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../plans/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md). Brain-analogy anti-drift: `working_memory` = pinned **charter** (frozen goal, written only by the goal authority) + **state** (executive-rewritten, safe-when-wrong); `anchoring` = pin (primacy) + tail (recency); `executive` = out-of-band state updater. Goal-authority seam = the world-model swap point for roleplay later.
> - **тЬЕ M1тАУM5** (prior commits 7335248eтАжa65ae5d8): contracts + `session_templates` (tenancy: System owner NULL / Per-user `UNIQUE(owner_user_id,code)`) + `working_memory_seed`; templates CRUD + frozen-charter seed; anchoring text+voice (shared helper, EC-3/EC-7); `working_memory` block as SSOT in knowledge-service; the `executive` worker (state-only, `covered` monotonic, charter immutable by construction тАФ no `update_charter` exists). M5 **behaviorally proven** with real local Qwen2.5 7B.
> - **тЬЕ M6 тАФ `/evaluate` scorecard (commit `53b832bd`).** `POST /v1/chat/sessions/{id}/evaluate` тАФ non-agentic pipeline scoring transcript + final `working_memory` + template rubric тЖТ structured `Scorecard` (STAR/clarity/filler/per-checklist/tips), stored as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. Coercion safe-when-wrong: per-checklist verdict rebuilt from `charter.checklist` (model can't drop/invent), scores clamped, `partial` server-set (EC-13). EC-10 no-model тЖТ 409. Portable (no `response_format` тАФ lm_studio quirk). 23 tests; chat-service suite 442 green.
> - **тЬЕ M7 тАФ seed + frontend (commit `<pending>`).** 3 System templates seeded via the chat-service migration (idempotent `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on the partial unique index; admin/platform path тАФ users clone, never mutate System). FE `features/interview/` (React-MVC): `useInterviewSetup`/`useEvaluation` hooks (controllers), `PersonaPicker`/`EndEvaluateBar`/`ScorecardView` views (render-only, props-driven), `InterviewPage` **reuses** the chat `ChatView` (text + voice, **no fork**; embedded mode, never unmounted) + scorecard overlay. Route `/interview` + sidebar entry (en/vi nav). 9 FE tests; typecheck/lint clean.
> - **тЬЕ LIVE-SMOKE (cross-service, real Qwen2.5 7B):** templates list via API тЖТ start practice тЖТ real transcript тЖТ `POST /evaluate` тЖТ **201 in 7.7s**, scorecard checklist rebuilt to exactly the 4 charter items (model marked 2 covered / 2 not, matching the transcript), `partial=True`, persisted as a `scorecard` ChatOutput. M6 now proven-live, same bar as M5. (`scratchpad/m6_evaluate_smoke.py`.)
> - **тЦ╢ NEXT:** **POST-REVIEW #3 (human checkpoint) тАФ present + WAIT.** Then optional **browser smoke** (test account: `/interview` тЖТ pick persona тЖТ answer a couple turns тЖТ End & evaluate тЖТ scorecard). After approval: merge `feat/interview-roleplay` тЖТ main (PR). The chat-service image was rebuilt+recreated this session with M6+M7 code (live-verified in container).
> - **Deferred (tracked):** **`D-PROVIDER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT`** (architecture C тАФ gate: out-of-scope, cross-service / new feature) тАФ provider-registry should NORMALIZE structured-output (canonical intent тЖТ per-provider dialect; prefer `json_schema`, accepted by BOTH OpenAI and lm_studio) instead of passthrough-ing raw `response_format` (which 400s `json_object` on lm_studio). Until then, LLM-JSON callers omit `response_format` + parse robustly (executive + evaluate both do). Target: a dedicated provider-registry branch. See memory `provider-structured-output-quirk`.
> - **Branch state:** test account `019d5e3c-тАж` (claude-test@loreweave.dev / Claude@Test2026) drives real local LLM smokes (Qwen2.5 7B `019eb620-тАж`). Stack up: chat-service :8212 + knowledge-service :8216 healthy (current code in containers); lm_studio :1234.

# тЦ╢ PRIOR (merged to main via PR #43) тАФ agent-journey BATCH cleared (M1 gateway X-Project-Id ┬╖ M2 wiki freq-gate ┬╖ M3 enrich owner-gate ┬╖ M4 compose poll-tool) ┬╖ branch `feat/knowledge-graph-ontology` ┬╖ 2026-06-22

> **JUST DONE тАФ the 4 backend agent-journey deferrals are CLEARED (plan: [2026-06-22-agent-journey-gaps-batch.md](../plans/2026-06-22-agent-journey-gaps-batch.md)), live-proven through the gateway on the fresh book (`019eef55тАж`, project `019eef5dтАж`):**
> 1. **тЬЕ M1 `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`** тАФ ai-gateway federation now forwards `X-Project-Id`. Added `projectId` to `Envelope`, read in `extractEnvelope`/`extractAdminEnvelope`, forwarded in `executeTool`/`adminHeaders` ([federation.service.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/federation/federation.service.ts), [handlers.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/mcp/handlers.ts), [admin-handlers.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/mcp/admin-handlers.ts), [admin-federation.service.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/federation/admin-federation.service.ts)). **Live: `kg_build_graph` + `kg_build_wiki` now MINT through the gateway (`no_project_in_scope=False`) тАФ the exact calls that failed pre-fix.** Tests: handlers.spec + admin-federation.spec (27 green); TSC clean.
> 2. **тЬЕ M2 `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`** (renamed тАФ CORRECTS the prior "draft-vs-active" framing) тАФ `kg_build_wiki` found 0 entities on a 1-chapter book because `_resolve_entity_ids` called `known-entities` at the **default `min_frequency=2`**; the Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency`, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Fix: `list_entities` gains a `min_frequency` param; the wiki resolver passes `min_frequency=1` ([glossary_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/glossary_client.py), [build_wiki_effect.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/ontology/build_wiki_effect.py)). **Live DECISIVE proof (root cause settled): on the fresh book тАФ all 26 entities `status='draft'` тАФ `known-entities?status=active&min_frequency=1` тЖТ 26, `&min_frequency=2` тЖТ 0. Status is ignored; the gate was purely chapter-frequency, NOT draft-vs-active.** Tests: knowledge unit 31 green.
> 3. **тЬЕ M3 `D-ENRICH-MCP-OWNER-GATE`** тАФ wired a `GrantClient` shim into lore-enrichment (shared `loreweave_grants` SDK) + lifespan init/close + a **VIEW** grant gate in `auto_enrich` BEFORE the glossary read; fail-closed (book-service outage тЖТ NONE тЖТ 404, never an existence oracle). The MCP tool passes `grants=get_grant_client()` EXPLICITLY (it calls the handler as a plain fn тАФ `Depends` defaults don't resolve) ([grant_client.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/clients/grant_client.py), [gaps.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/api/gaps.py), [mcp/server.py](../../services/lore-enrichment-service/app/mcp/server.py)). **Live: through the gateway, a foreign book_id тЖТ `{success:false, status:404}`; the real owner resolves `grant_level=owner` (gate allows, no false-deny).** Tests: gaps_api + mcp_server 21 green.
> 4. **тЬЕ M4 `D-COMPOSE-GENERATE-WORKER-POLL`** тАФ added Tier-R `composition_get_generation_job` (mirrors `composition_get_prose`; user-scoped repo `.get` + a project-ownership assert so a foreign-project job_id can't be read through this Work) ([mcp/server.py](../../services/composition-service/app/mcp/server.py)). **Live: federated in the gateway catalogue (129 tools).** Tests: composition unit 43 green (owner-ok / foreign-project / missing).
> - **VERIFY (live, 4 rebuilt images, through the gateway):** combined smoke [scripts/smoke_journey_gaps_batch.py](../../scripts/smoke_journey_gaps_batch.py) тЖТ RESULT: PASS (M1 both kg tools mint, M3 foreign-book 404, M4 poll-tool federated) + the direct M2 freq-gate probe + the M3 owner/foreign `/access` probe. provider-gate + language-rule clean; ai-gateway TSC clean; lore-enrichment 979 tests collect (no import cycle).
> - **тЬЕ PAYOFF RUN DONE 2026-06-22 тАФ clean 9/9 agent-driven THROUGH THE GATEWAY** on the fresh book (drivers [run_99_through_gateway_67.py](../../scripts/run_99_through_gateway_67.py) + [_89.py](../../scripts/run_99_through_gateway_89.py)): **6 KG** `kg_build_graph` mintтЖТ`/v1/kg/actions/confirm`тЖТ`{started, running, scope:all}`; **7 wiki** `kg_build_wiki` mintтЖТconfirmтЖТ`{entity_count:26}` (pre-fix 422) тЖТ **wiki_articles 0тЖТ10+ produced** (M2 end-to-end, gemma local); **8 enrich** `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` (owner via gateway)тЖТ`{detected:26, enqueued_gaps:3}`тЖТ**3 `proposed` proposals**, synthesized not verbatim (Borgo Pass хЬ░чРЖ / Bistritz; M3 owner path, no false-deny); **9 write** `composition_generate`тЖТconfirmтЖТ**real gothic chapter persisted, draft_version 2, 2627 chars**. The KG/wiki direct-on-knowledge workaround is retired тАФ M1+M2 carry it through federation.
> - **тЬЕ KG-benchmark M4+M5 CLEARED 2026-06-22 (commit `e66a7308`):** surfaced `gate_failures` on `GET /benchmark-status` (from `raw_report`); `BenchmarkBadge` reason-aware copy (`insufficient_runs`тЖТ"inconclusive, needs тЙе3 passes"; never "low-quality" at good recall); `BuildGraphDialog` leading prereq checklist (LLMтЖТembeddingтЖТbenchmark тЬУ/тЬЧ/тЛп); `not_benchmark_project` toast de-obsoleted (sandbox) + model-swap warning; new `contracts/api/knowledge-service/benchmark.yaml` (benchmark-run/status + gate_failures + the `kg_run_benchmark` tool). 13 knowledge + 56 FE tests; tsc + spectral clean. (FE not browser-smoked тАФ well unit-covered; backend field unit-tested.)
> - **тЬЕ `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS` CLEARED 2026-06-22 (commit `7969a08b`):** the enrichment cost columns/fields were named `*_usd` but always held TOKENS (C1 ruling). Renamed end-to-end: DB `enrichment_job.*_cost_usd`/`max_spend_usd`тЖТ`*_tokens` (guarded in-place RENAME + retype NUMERIC(14,2) тАФ a token count overflows the old (10,4); resume reads new key w/ legacy fallback); API request `max_spend_usd`тЖТ`max_spend_tokens` (jobs/auto-enrich/compose/mcp), response already unit-neutral (fixed the drifted OpenAPI); FE sends `max_spend_tokens`, JobsPanel shows `tok` not `$`, i18n "(USD)"тЖТ"(tokens)" ├Ч4 locales. Also fixed a latent break (test_auto_enrich_api wasn't grant-stubbed after the M3 owner-gate). 934 unit + 280 FE tests; **LIVE: migration applied on the real DB (renamed+retyped, healthy); GET /jobs тЖТ 200 with real token costs (13994/11231/36)**.
> - **тЬЕ `D-KG-PASSAGE-BACKFILL` CLEARED 2026-06-23 (passage-creation gap, root-caused live):** running the full Dracula journey surfaced that **wiki produced 0 articles** (and enrichment 0 proposals) тАФ `skipped_no_grounding`, because the KG project had **0 `:Passage` nodes**. Root cause: passages are ingested by the `chapter.published` **event handler**, which SKIPS when no project/embedding exists yet ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py) lines 144/194); the **natural flow publishes chapters BEFORE the KG project + embedding model exist**, so the publish event fires too early and **nothing backfills** (`backfill-orders` only re-indexes existing passages). Proven via `index-drafts` (on-demand ingest тЖТ 8 passages where the journey made 0). **Fix:** `backfill_published_passages` ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)) ingests passages for all PUBLISHED chapters as canon, wired best-effort into `PUT /embedding-model` ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py) тАФ the moment passages become ingestable; idempotent; a model CHANGE re-ingests at the new dim). 24 ingester + 35 embedding-model + 76 passage/event/raw-search tests; provider-gate clean. **LIVE: natural flow (publishтЖТprojectтЖТembedding) now backfills 6 canon `:Passage` nodes** where it made 0 before (`verify_passage_backfill.py`). NOTE surfaced: `book_chapter_publish` is proposeтЖТconfirm тАФ the journey drivers never confirmed it, so chapters stayed `draft` (extraction reads drafts; wiki needs published+grounded).
> - **тЬЕ END-TO-END PAYOFF RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (with a CONFIRMED publish):** fresh book `019ef35c` / project `019ef35e` тАФ natural flow (confirm-publish тЖТ create project тЖТ set embedding тЖТ backfill **6 passages** тЖТ KG build тЖТ wiki тЖТ enrich). **Wiki 0тЖТ20 articles ┬╖ Enrichment 0тЖТ3 proposals (job completed, 30,131 `actual_cost_tokens`) ┬╖ chapter written (2330 chars)** тАФ the two grounding-dependent steps that were 0 now produce real output. Also caught + fixed a stale `lore-enrichment-worker` (pre-rename image тЖТ `column actual_cost_usd does not exist`); recreating it fixed enrichment. **Deploy note: after a schema rename, recreate worker containers too, not just the API service.**
> - **тЬЕ MERGED `origin/main` 2026-06-23 (merge commit `b8070be6`) тАФ branch now 0 behind main.** PR #42 (extraction-knowledge-architecture) reconciled most prior defers (`D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES`, `D-EXTRACTION-ADMISSION-CONTROL`, `D-CACHE-MODEL-KEY`, `D-KG-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT`тАж). Conflict resolution: ledger.go renumbered this branch's migration `0035тЖТ0036` (collided with main's `0035_multirow`); 4 translation extraction files took main (superset: grant-clamped effort + planner-wiring); 2 hidden auto-merge artifacts fixed (duplicate `reasoning_effort` kwarg in `extraction_worker.py` sig + `actions.py` call); 2 HEAD tests aligned to main's API. VERIFY post-merge: py_compile all services+SDKs; Go glossary+book build; translation extraction 42 + knowledge 45 + lore-enrichment 39 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **тЦ╢ NEXT / BRANCH STATUS:** **all of this branch's deferrals are CLEARED.** The only tracked-open item is **`D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`** тАФ XL, **pre-existing on main** (NOT this branch's debt), **blocked on `world-core-foundation`**, design ready тАФ it carries forward as main's existing debt. A couple of live-smoke rows (`D-S5-LIVE-SMOKE` needs a BYOK web-search key) are infra gaps, not code defects. **Branch is ready to PR into main.**

# Session Handoff тАФ `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` ┬╖ **EXTRACTION RE-ARCH тАФ FND+RE+PROV+OBS SHIPPED; MERGE/PLAN/CACHE remain** ┬╖ (prior: design complete; `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` тАФ 26/26, PR #39) ┬╖ 2026-06-22

Ask the user which.**
> - **тЬЕ D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 2 тАФ planner feasibility gate (commit `a37c0527`; DESIGN [2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md](../specs/2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md), Option B).** KEY REFRAME: `plan()` is a token-budget STRUCTURE planner (estimates, not text payloads) тЖТ it CAN'T replace the windower (which makes prose); its value is the `Unplannable` signal. So Part 2 wires it as a **pre-flight feasibility GATE** over the existing windowing, NOT a replacement: build a Unit per (window├Чbatch), run `plan()`, and any unit it refuses (an oversized block that can't fit even alone) тЖТ record a new `unplannable` batch-outcome + **skip its LLM call** (no truncation, no tokens) тЖТ chapter derives `completed_with_errors` (INV-F15) so the block is VISIBLE. The unit id IS the `{window_idx}:{batch_idx}` coordinate тЗТ **zero remap** onto the cache-gate/OBS/provenance seams (my earlier "structural XL" fear was wrong тАФ the gate touches none of them). Fires only when input won't fit with the minimum output reservation = exactly when the executor would truncate (no false-positive skip тАФ a flagged window couldn't extract anyway). Best-effort: any planner failure тЖТ ungated (prior path). New OBS taxonomy `unplannable` (distinct from `truncated`). VERIFY: 83 extraction unit tests + **FULL LIVE E2E** (`D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` тАФ see below); provider-gate clean.
> - **тЬЕ D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE (unplannable path) тАФ DONE live on the real stack.** Auth (test acct) тЖТ created a 348k-char single-block chapter in the Dracula book тЖТ `POST /v1/extraction/.../extract-glossary` тЖТ worker тЖТ **`extraction_batch_outcomes.status='unplannable'`** + chapter `completed_with_errors`, job done in <1s with **NO LLM call**. Clean before/after: the SAME job on the pre-gate image produced `llm_error` (the oversized window hit `LLMInvalidRequest` context-overflow тАФ the exact S1тАУS5 failure); the gated worker produced `unplannable` (graceful skip). **GOTCHA caught (memory `docker-stale-copy-layer-false-smoke`):** the first rebuild served a STALE app COPY layer (old code, no gate) while the SDK layer was fresh тАФ so my initial "in-container `plan()` snippet" smoke was a FALSE pass (it tested the SDK, not the worker's gate). Fixed with `build --no-cache` + `up --force-recreate`; re-verified the gate code is in the container before re-running. Both translation images are now rebuilt `--no-cache` (current code live). Test chapter cleaned up. The broader full-pipeline-WITH-entities E2E (needs a live LLM producing entities) remains nice-to-have, not blocking.
> - **тЬЕ CACHE D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 1 тАФ split-aware cost estimate (commit `30c7549d`).** `estimate_extraction_cost` ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py)) now models each (chapter ├Ч kind-batch) as a `chunk`-splittable planner Unit (`max_units_per_call=1` so the per-unit input sum == real per-call input тАФ extraction injects window text PER batch call, never shared) and splits oversized chapters into windows, so the quote tracks the executor's real fan-out (+ surfaces `unplannable`/`model_fit_warning`). Extracted the worker's context resolver into shared [extraction_model.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_model.py); the HTTP route + MCP start-extraction tool resolve the REAL model context and thread it in (so the quote uses the SAME budget the executor will). ANY planner failure (ImportError or plan() error) degrades to the flat heuristic тАФ a cost estimate must never 500 job creation. VERIFY: 4 cost + worker 11 + mcp 36 + extraction subset 32 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **тЬЕ CACHE Slice 3a тАФ retention (commit `36193284`, not pushed).** `purge_stale_raw_outputs` ([extraction_cache.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_cache.py)) keeps the latest K content-hash GENERATIONS per (owner, book, chapter) + purges older (cache growth bounded by edit-history depth, not edit count) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/retention` (the scheduler seam). Destructive-op safety: current gen is always rank-1 (never purged even at keepтЖТfloored-1); rows join on content_hash so a gen is never half-purged; DENSE_RANK over-keeps on ties; partition keyed by owner_user_id (INV-9). VERIFY (live PG): keep-K / scope / tenant-isolation test; 66 extraction tests green; provider-gate clean.
> - **тЬЕ CACHE Slice 3b тАФ replay (commit `0f097422`, not pushed).** New [extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py) `replay_chapter_from_cache` re-drives the glossary writeback from a chapter's CACHED parse at **$0 LLM** (recovery: failed writeback / cleared entities / re-materialize) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/replay`. Faithful-by-construction: proceeds only when CURRENT text still hashes to the cached `content_hash` AND the source job's profile still hashes to the cached `profile_hash` (else no_cache / profile_unavailable тЖТ fresh extraction) тАФ so re-stamped evidence offsets + links + attribute-actions are all valid. Gating: grant (EDIT on book, asserted-user re-verified), tenancy (reads ONLY caller's own cache, writes caller-attributed тАФ INV-9), confirm (confirm=False тЖТ dry-run PREVIEW no write; confirm=True тЖТ idempotent writeback w/ the worker's writeback_key). **/review-impl (self): 1 MED** (duplicated `_merge_window_entities` тЖТ import the worker's single source of truth тАФ guards a future merge-key drift) **+ 1 LOW** (writeback failure тЖТ distinct `writeback_failed` status). VERIFY (live PG): 5 replay tests (preview-no-write / confirm-writes-merged / content-drift / profile-unavailable / tenant-isolation) + 3 cache + extraction+dispatch subset green; provider-gate clean.

>   - **тЦ╢ RE follow-ups (tracked, defer-eligible):** тЬЕ **`D-RE-WORKER-GRADED-EFFORT` CLEARED 2026-06-22** тАФ the extraction worker now honors graded `reasoning_effort` (low/medium/high), no longer collapsing to medium-or-none. Threaded through `CreateExtractionJobPayload` тЖТ `job_params` + the `extraction.job` publish msg тЖТ worker read+validate (out-of-vocab тЖТ bool fallback) тЖТ `_process_extraction_chapter` тЖТ `effort_band_for(thinking_enabled, reasoning_effort)` (cache band) + the gateway wire fields via the shared SDK `reasoning_fields(ReasoningDirective(...))` (replaces the local `thinking_llm_fields` medium-or-none in extraction; the bool stays the back-compat alias TrueтЗТmedium). Files: [extraction.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py) + [actions.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/actions.py) + [extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py). Tests: +3 (graded-high / explicit-none-disables-thinking / bool-fallback-medium assert the exact gateway `input`), 103 green (worker+cache+mcp+grant+extraction); provider-gate clean. **LIVE-SMOKE caught + fixed a real bug the mocks missed:** a first attempt persisted `reasoning_effort` into a non-existent `extraction_jobs` column (that column lives on `extraction_raw_outputs`/the CACHE ledger) тЖТ 500 on job create; reverted (the worker consumes effort from the publish msg + the cache already records it тАФ no `extraction_jobs` column needed). Re-smoke: `reasoning_effort=low` flowed to gemma and turned thinking ON (`finish=length`, output budget burned on hidden reasoning) тАФ proof the field reaches the model (extraction's DEFAULT path is thinking-OFF, unchanged). **Note:** any low/medium/high enables thinking тЖТ a weak local model can over-think extraction into truncation; default (none) stays clean тАФ pairs with `D-RE-EFFORT-COST-ESTIMATE`. тЬЕ **`D-RE-EFFORT-COST-ESTIMATE` CLEARED 2026-06-22** тАФ `estimate_extraction_cost` now scales the OUTPUT reservation by `reasoning_effort` (`_EFFORT_OUTPUT_MULTIPLIER` none/off=1.0, low=1.5, medium=2.5, high=4.0 тАФ reasoning tokens count toward output), threaded from BOTH callers (the HTTP/confirm create path [extraction.py] + the MCP mint estimate [mcp/server.py]). So a high-effort quote no longer under-represents spend (the clamp ceiling was already enforced; this fixes the *estimate*). Input tokens unaffected; unknown effort degrades to 1.0. +1 unit test (monotonic none<low<high, omitted==none, garbage==baseline); 40 green (windowing+mcp); provider-gate clean. `D-RE-OTHER-AGENTIC-EFFORT` (deep-research + translation MCP tools should adopt the same param+clamp). **RE lane essentially complete** (chat no-op fix + resolver + /no_think + MCP param + effort-auth); these are polish.

> - **тЬЕ lane PLAN (planner SDK) тАФ two-phase split/pack.** New [planner.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_extraction/planner.py) in `loreweave_extraction` (reuses `ContextBudget`/`estimate_text_tokens`; exported from the package). `plan(PlanRequest)` is PURE + two-phase (architecture ┬з3.1, ┬з8.4): **Phase 1 normalize/split** тАФ a unit whose est input/output exceeds the per-call budget is split along its `split_axis` (chunkтЖТkindтЖТattr); irreducible тЖТ `Unplannable` (surfaced to the cost-gate, never silently truncated тАФ the S1тАУS5 fix). **Phase 2 pack** тАФ fit in-budget units up to the budget AND `max_units_per_call`. `per_call_budget` scales the output reservation by `reasoning_effort` (`effort_output_multiplier`, composes the reasoning spec); each `LLMCall` carries stable `unit_ids` for 1:1 VALIDATE attribution (┬з8.4.6); **fan-out guard** (`model_fit_warning` when one unit explodes past `fan_out_warn_threshold`); **cost range** low/expected/high with INJECTED pricing (no hardcoded price/model). VERIFY: planner 11 + budget 35 green; provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: no HIGH/MED** (1 LOW тАФ `expansion_ratio>1` can mark a tight sub-unit Unplannable rather than split once more; safe тАФ surfaces, never truncates). **Consumer = CACHE** (the executor): the worker's `plan_kind_batches` is unchanged; PLAN is wired into the executor when CACHE lands (the plan's "CACHE needs PLAN").
