<details><summary>Design-complete handoff (FND now built тАФ kept for context)</summary>

> **тЦ╢ extraction/LLM-pipeline re-architecture, BUILD via the parallel plan.** Branch `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` (off the glossary work). The 26-scenario test surfaced 5 architectural gaps; this session did the **full design**, now COMPLETE and build-ready:
> - **Design docs (committed):** [architecture rev 2](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-architecture.md) (┬з7тАУ┬з11 = a 4-lens adversarial evaluation: concurrency/failure/scale/tenancy, with the scenario matrix + hardening contracts), [reasoning-effort](../specs/2026-06-21-reasoning-effort-control.md), [**detailed design**](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-detailed-design.md) (every open question RESOLVED тАФ decisions table ┬з1 + DDL ┬з2 + interfaces ┬з3 + 15 enforceable invariants ┬з4), [**parallel plan**](../plans/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-plan.md).
> - **The 5 gaps тЖТ one missing layer:** an explicit `PLAN тЖТ EXECUTE+CACHE тЖТ VALIDATE тЖТ WRITEBACK тЖТ OBSERVE` spine. P1 observability (services exist, unwired), P2 unified planner (scattered across 5+ modules), P3 evidence provenance (columns exist, unpopulated), P4 merge/append policy (no append, silent skips), P5 raw cache (spec'd, was deferred).
> - **Key design verdicts to honor in BUILD:** the **two-ledger model** (raw-output cache тЙа writeback-coverage log тАФ else a failed writeback permanently masks un-landed entities = data-loss amplifier); **concurrency contract** (glossary has NO `UNIQUE(book,kind,name)` today тЖТ concurrent jobs dup; need constraint + per-book advisory lock + transactional whole-chapter writeback + idempotency key + content-hash precondition); **batch-outcome taxonomy** (empty_valid тЙа validation_rejected тЙа truncated тАФ the literal 26-scenario bug); **two-phase planner** (split-down, not just pack-up; extraction has no chapter-chunking); **provenance trust** (model offsets are INV-6 untrusted, validate not trust); **verified-clobber guard** (merge never overwrites human-verified); **tenancy** (owner_user_id + grant gate + INV-6 + redaction on every new table/event). 4 HIGH findings are design-blocking тЖТ resolved in the detailed design.
> - **Build = 7 parallel lanes (plan ┬з2):** FND (M0 finish_reason тЖТ M1 concurrency/two-ledger тАФ the blocker), RE (reasoning-effort + the wiring no-op fix), PLAN (planner SDK), OBS, PROV, MERGE, CACHE. Critical path FNDтЖТPROVтЖТMERGE; RE+PLAN start at minute 0. `extraction_handler.go` is the hot file тАФ FNDтЖТPROVтЖТMERGE sequence on it, never concurrent.
> - **Gate decision (PO-reversible):** Option A тАФ pipeline-first, build in translation+glossary now, cache behind an interface (physical re-home to knowledge deferred = `D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`). 3 tracked deferrals: that + `D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES` + `D-RAWCACHE-MINIO-OFFLOAD`.
> - **First BUILD action next session:** start lane **FND** (M0 finish_reason propagation, then M1) + lane **RE** in parallel. The RE wiring fix (chat thinking toggle is a live no-op today) is a standalone user-visible win.

</details>

# тЦ╢ Dracula end-to-end JOURNEY verification (live, 2026-06-22) тАФ steps 1тАУ8 PROVEN; 9тАУ11 remain

> **The real product scenario, driven live on the actual Dracula book** (`019eeb09`, test account) instead of clearing micro-deferrals. Journey: create book тЖТ agent web-search+suggest ontology тЖТ approve тЖТ import тЖТ extract glossary тЖТ translate glossary+chapter to vi тЖТ build KG тЖТ build wiki тЖТ enrich тЖТ write chapter.
> - **тЬЕ 1 Create book / тЬЕ 2 Agent-suggested ontology (book-optimized: `vampire/hunter/victim/werewolf/witch/artifact` + defaults) / тЬЕ 3 Approve (12 kinds, 52 attrs adopted) / тЬЕ 4 Import (**4 published chapters now** тАФ fetched the full public-domain novel from Gutenberg #345 since the repo ships only ch01, split it, bulk-ingested ch2тАУ4 via `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk`; a stray `OVERSIZED unplannable E2E` draft sits at sort 99) / тЬЕ 5 Extract glossary (30 entities) / тЬЕ 6 GlossaryтЖТvi (233 attr translations) / тЬЕ 7 ChapterтЖТvi (`vi/completed`).** All verified by live DB state, not handoff claims.
> - **тЬЕ 8 Build KG тАФ DONE this session (was the key blocker).** Drove `POST /v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/extraction/{estimate,start}` live. **Neo4j now holds the Dracula graph: 65 Entity (Count Dracula, Jonathan Harker, Castle Dracula, Carpathians, LondonтАж), 17 Event, 3 Fact, 10 Passage, 15 ExtractionSource + 60 EVIDENCED_BY + 21 RELATES_TO** (job complete, 65 items, $0.06, gemma + bge-m3, scope=all). **ЁЯФ┤ BLOCKER SURFACED (real UX gap тЖТ fix): build-KG is gated behind the K17.9 embedding benchmark, which had NEVER been run here** (0 benchmark runs). The non-obvious trip: `min_runs: 3` тАФ a `runs=1` benchmark returns `passed:false` DESPITE perfect recall@3=1.0/mrr=1.0 (bge-m3 passes cleanly at runs=3; 1024-dim neg-control override 0.70 applied). The agent's `kg_build_graph` mints a token fine but extraction 409s `benchmark_missing` until the user benchmarks their embedding model тАФ the journey never surfaces this prerequisite. Also: changing the project embedding model returns `extraction_status:disabled` + deletes prior nodes (vector-space reset). **`D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX`** (tracked; ANALYSIS DONE тЖТ [docs/specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md](../specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md)). **Verdict: primarily a DESIGN gap (an unreconciled contradiction) + 2 implementation gaps.** The benchmark WAS designed as a visible "Run benchmark" step gating Confirm (RAID brief `05_build-graph-gates.md`, UX review P0 KN-1/BL-16) and the FE wires that button тАФ but: **(a) DESIGN contradiction** тАФ the gate keys benchmark to `(user, project, embedding_model)` (extraction.py:696) and the FE button is on the REAL project, yet the runner REFUSES any project that already has chapter/chat/glossary passages (`runner.py:223-227` `not_benchmark_project`); all three can't hold once the project has content, so on a non-trivial project the UI's only remedy structurally can't clear the gate (the RAID brief scoped "NO backend changes" assuming the endpoint fit тАФ it didn't). I only cleared it because change-embedding had just emptied the project. **(b) IMPL** тАФ `core.py:159` short-circuits `runs<min_runs` BEFORE metrics тЖТ `runs=1` returns `passed:false` at `recall@3=1.0` with no reason + a misleading FE "low-quality results" badge (FE always sends runs:3, so only API/agent callers hit it тАФ i.e. exactly my drive). **(c) DESIGN** тАФ a successful run leaves ~10 synthetic `benchmark_entity` passages, no cleanup (pollution). **(d) IMPL** тАФ `kg_build_graph` warns but there's NO `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool тЖТ agent dead-end. **Fix breakdown:** R1 decouple benchmarkтЖТ(user,embedding_model) on an auto-sandbox (root cause, needs a plan + contract change); R2 add `fail_reason` + stop FE mislabelling a perfect short run; R3 clamp/reject `runs<min_runs` on the interactive endpoint; R4 add `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (MCP-first). R2+R3 are fix-now-small; R1+R4 need a plan. **тЦ╢ BATCH-FIX BUILD (plan: [2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md](../plans/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md)) тАФ BACKEND M1тАУM3 DONE 2026-06-22 (commits 7f3502af, ccf52484, 0d8306e2, 17c89fa0):** **M1/R1** re-keyed the gate to `(user, embedding_model)` (`BenchmarkRunsRepo.get_latest_for_model`) + routed the run to a hidden per-(user,model) sandbox (`get_or_create_benchmark_sandbox`; migration `is_benchmark_sandbox`; list-excluded) тАФ **live-proven:** ran the benchmark on the content-bearing Dracula project (65 entities) тЖТ passed, **NO `not_benchmark_project`** (old code 409s), sandbox created with the fixtures, real project's `benchmark_entity` count stayed 10 (**zero new pollution**), sandbox absent from list API. **M2/R2+R3** `gate_failures()` named codes (`insufficient_runs` тЙа a quality fail) threaded to the response/to_json + the runner clamps `runs` up to `min_runs` тАФ **live-proven:** `runs=1` тЖТ `passed=True, runs=3, gate_failures=[]` (the perfect-but-failed trap is gone). **M3/R4** `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (handler + arg model + definition + shim; OWNER-only, direct, runs on the sandbox) тАФ catalogue unit-green (TOOL_DEFINITIONS 26). Full knowledge unit suite **2988 green**; provider-gate clean. **REMAINING: M4 (FE) + M5 (contract)** тАФ R2 badge copy (`insufficient_runs` тЖТ "inconclusive, needs тЙе3 runs" not "low-quality"), R5 `BuildGraphDialog` prerequisite checklist, R6 model-swap warning copy + the OpenAPI `gate_failures`/tool additions. The FE is isolated copy/checklist on a now-working backend; the journey's KG-build dead-end is ALREADY cleared by the backend.
> - **тЬЕ 9 Build wiki тАФ DONE 2026-06-22.** Drove `POST /internal/knowledge/books/{id}/wiki/generate` (S2S) for 3 entities тЖТ **3 wiki articles generated** (Jonathan Harker / Count Dracula / Mina; 2.3тАУ5KB bodies, `generation_status=needs_review` from the local-model verify pass), job complete, $0.15 via gemma. **ЁЯФ┤ GATE SURFACED: wiki-gen is flag-gated OFF** (`WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` тЖТ the `wiki_gen_processor` consumer never runs, job sits `pending` forever); had to restart knowledge-service with `KNOWLEDGE_WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=true`. Another un-surfaced journey prerequisite (like the KG benchmark) тЖТ **`D-JOURNEY-WIKI-FLAG` тЬЕ FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9):** the consumer now ALWAYS runs (it's idle/free until a user-triggered, cost-gated job arrives тАФ gating it off-by-default only produced silent eternal pends); spend stays bounded per-request (`max_spend_usd` + the cost-gated trigger), not a platform env. `wiki_gen_enabled` deprecatedтЖТdefault True, no longer gates the consumer. **Live-proven:** restarted knowledge-service with `WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` тЖТ the wiki-gen consumer still started.
> - **тЬЕ 10 Enrichment тАФ DROVE IT + FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9).** `POST /v1/lore-enrichment/projects/{id}/auto-enrich` тЖТ scanned 30 entities, detected 30 gaps. **The user's call was right тАФ gemma is fine, the CODE was weak; 3 code bugs found + fixed (NOT a model problem):** **(A) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-EMPTY-COMPLETION`** тАФ one empty LLM completion raised `CompletionSeamError` that escaped the per-gap handler and **FAILED the whole job**, discarding the gaps that worked. Now caught as a per-gap skip (runner.py). **(C) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-VERBATIM`** тАФ the gen prompt said "use ONLY the excerpts"+"don't invent" but never "synthesize", so gemma safely **copied the source verbatim**, which our OWN anti-plagiarism gate then auto-rejected (self-defeating loop); both language templates now demand paraphrase/re-creation (generate.py). **(B) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS`** тАФ `spent=8409` is **8409 TOKENS not $8409** (the cap is token-denominated per the C1 PO ruling, but columns/logs say `*_cost_usd`); relabelled the log, the DB/API `*_usd`тЖТtokens rename is structural (migration+FE) and tracked. **Live-proven (re-ran after the fix):** job **COMPLETED** (was `failed`), **4 proposals `review_status='proposed'` NOT `rejected`** тАФ gemma now PARAPHRASES (Borgo Pass хЬ░чРЖ"ш┐ЮцОецпФцЦпчЙ╣щЗМшМиф╕Ох╕ГчзСч╗┤ч║│чЪДх▒▒щЧ┤ш╖пх╛Д" тАФ synthesized, not the verbatim English source), clearing the gate. Confidence 0.30 is the H0 quarantine floor (awaiting human review), not a rejection.
> - **тЬЕ 11 Write new chapter тАФ DRIVEN AGENT-SIDE 2026-06-22 via the new `composition_generate` MCP tool.** Through the gateway: `composition_create_work` тЖТ `composition_outline_node_create` (chapter+scene; both kinds require a real book `chapter_id` per the `outline_chapter_required` constraint, so a new book chapter `019eef4c` was created first) тЖТ `composition_generate` (propose, mints a `composition.generate` token) тЖТ confirm (`/v1/composition/actions/confirm`) тЖТ the confirm effect ran `engine.generate_chapter(persist=True)` IN-PROCESS тЖТ **gemma drafted a full gothic chapter (real Stoker-voice prose) тЖТ PERSISTED to the book draft (draft_version 2), status `completed`.** (`grounding=False`/`canon=skipped_no_cast` because the minimal scene carried no `present_entity_ids` тЖТ voice-only grounding; a scene with cast would pull KG/glossary canon.) **The grounded cowrite ENGINE is now agent-reachable.** Step 10 was likewise re-driven agent-side via the new `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` MCP tool (job `019eef45` тЖТ 2 synthesized `proposed` proposals: Borgo Pass / Bistritz, paraphrased not verbatim).
> - **Scenario status:** the 2 tool-gaps are closed and BOTH new tools are real-execution-proven agent-driven on the Dracula book. **FULL FRESH-BOOK AGENT-DRIVEN RUN done 2026-06-22** (fresh book `019eef55тАж`, KG project `019eef5dтАж`, all through the gateway MCP surface unless noted):
>   - тЬЕ **1 create** (`book_create`) ┬╖ тЬЕ **2 ontology** (`glossary_web_search` + baseline adopted + `vampire`+`hunter` custom kinds via `glossary_propose_new_kind`тЖТglossary confirm; verified in `book_kinds`) ┬╖ тЬЕ **3 import** (`book_chapter_bulk_create` real Dracula ch.1 + publish) ┬╖ тЬЕ **4 extract** (`translation_start_extraction` proposeтЖТconfirm тЖТ **26 entities**) ┬╖ тЬЕ **5 translateтЖТvi** (`translation_start_job` proposeтЖТconfirm тЖТ completed).
>   - тЬЕ **6 KG build** тАФ `kg_build_graph` proposeтЖТ`/v1/kg/actions/confirm` тЖТ **26 Entity nodes in Neo4j** for project `019eef5dтАж`, `extraction_status=ready` (benchmark via REST `benchmark-run` PASSED, embedding model via PATCH тАФ config steps with no MCP surface). **ЁЯФ┤ NEW GATEWAY GAP `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`:** project-scoped KG tools resolve the project from `X-Project-Id`, but **the ai-gateway federation DROPS `X-Project-Id`** тЖТ through the gateway they 'no project in scope'; DIRECT to knowledge `/mcp` (X-Project-Id set) they mint fine. So KG/wiki were driven **direct on knowledge `/mcp`** (still MCP tool-calls; the new composition/enrich tools are immune тАФ they take `project_id`/ids as explicit args). Re-confirmed `D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX` (fresh project тЖТ embedding set тЖТ `extraction_status=disabled` until the benchmark runs; `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool absent from the deployed gateway catalog).
>   - тЧР **7 wiki** тАФ `kg_build_wiki` propose mints + the confirm reaches the effect, but **422 "no entities тАж extract the glossary first"**. **тЪая╕П ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED 2026-06-22 + FIXED (M2 above, `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`):** this was NOT draft-vs-active. The `known-entities` Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency` (default **2**); the wiki effect called it at the default, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Proven live: all 26 are `draft`, yet `status=active&min_frequency=1` тЖТ 26 and `&min_frequency=2` тЖТ 0. (The OLD Dracula book worked because it has 4 chapters тЖТ freq тЙе2, not because its entities were active.) Wiki resolver now passes `min_frequency=1`.
>   - тЬЕ **8 enrich** (`lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` тАФ the NEW tool, **through the gateway**) тЖТ real synthesized proposal on the fresh book once KG embeddings existed (e.g. Borgo Pass хЬ░чРЖ/features, `review_status=proposed`). First pass before KG produced 0 (retrieval needs the book embeddings the KG build creates). ┬╖ тЬЕ **9 write** (`composition_generate` тАФ the NEW tool тАФ proposeтЖТconfirm тЖТ **real gothic chapter generated + persisted to the fresh book draft, draft_version 2**).
>   - **Verdict (original run): 8/9 driven; the 2 blockers (X-Project-Id drop + wiki freq-gate) were then ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED in the batch above, and the journey RE-RUN clean 9/9 through the gateway (see the тЬЕ PAYOFF RUN line in the NEXT block).** Non-gateway-MCP steps in the original run were config-only setup (embedding-model pick, benchmark) + the KG/wiki tools (then blocked by `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`, now forwarded). **All four agent tools (`kg_build_graph`, `kg_build_wiki`, `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich`, `composition_generate`) now work cleanly through the gateway end-to-end.**
> - **Note:** running the benchmark on the real Dracula project (user-approved) loaded golden-set fixtures into it; the post-extraction graph still reads as clean Dracula entities.

VERIFY: editor test 3/3, book-tabs 12/12, `tsc --noEmit` clean. >   - **Approve flow was never actually exercised before** тАФ the agent *proposed* book-optimized kinds (Vampire/Hunter/Victim/Artifact + edges) but they were never approved, so the book showed only the 6 adopted defaults. Drove proposeтЖТapprove live: 4 book-native kinds + attributes (with descriptions) now in `book_kinds`/`book_attributes`; patch-defaults + delete-unneeded also proven (DB-verified). There IS an automated approve-flow test (`action_confirm_test.go` mints `descSchemaCreateKind` тЖТ confirm тЖТ asserts `book_kinds` row).

Now: every kind gets 3-6 attributes, every attribute gets a specific description, curate-adopted-defaults guidance, and the `glossary_book_patch` FLAT arg-shape (weak models defaulted to the entity-edit `changes` diff тЖТ silent no-op). Live-verified proactive (hunter: methods/allegiance/notable_kills/motivation, each described).
>   - **ЁЯФ┤ GUI approve gap FIXED (the headline) тАФ `D-GLOSSARY-AUTOCONFIRM`** тАФ the local model mints the confirm_token but never calls the frontend `glossary_confirm_action` tool, so NO Approve card rendered (only prose "confirm in your dashboard"); a GUI-only user couldn't approve. The FE rendered the card ONLY from a suspended frontend tool. Fix: `AssistantMessage` now AUTO-renders the confirm card from any completed propose result carrying a LIVE confirm_token (reuses ConfirmCard/ConfirmActionCard, synthetic record, resume no-ops, exp-decoded to skip stale replay, deduped vs explicit). Plus the live SSE path now keeps the tool `result` so the card shows during the turn, not only after reload. (Caught only because the user insisted on a REAL in-GUI test тАФ prior API-POST approvals had masked it.)

> - **тЬЕ Extraction-quality & approve-flow fixes тАФ F2 + F3 BUILT (2026-06-22).** Spec [`docs/specs/2026-06-22-glossary-kg-extraction-quality-fixes.md`](../specs/2026-06-22-glossary-kg-extraction-quality-fixes.md) (CLARIFY-signed-off). Commits: `4a77e864` (F2a/F2b), `245344f3` (F3a), `830b33dd` (F3b), `7a406a20` (F2c/F3c).
>   - **F2 тАФ KG schema approve fixed.** The auto-confirm card mis-routed `kg_`-prefixed (non-dotted) descriptors to the glossary endpoint (422). `descriptorDomain` now maps `kg_*` тЖТ the `kg` domain тЖТ ConfirmActionCard тЖТ `/v1/kg/actions/*`; added a GET `/v1/kg/actions/preview?token=` alias so the shared card previews KG like every domain (BFF already proxies `/v1/kg/*`). +7 tests. Adopt-first guidance added to the knowledge skill (F2c).
>   - **F3a тАФ tolerant `glossary_book_patch`.** Accepts the entity-edit `changes[]` diff shape weak models kept emitting (silent no-op before) and folds it onto the flat fields (nil-only; drops a hallucinated base_version). +4 tests.
>   - **F3b тАФ one-shot `glossary_propose_new_kind` with `attributes[]` (the headline reliability win).** The kind + its attributes are created in ONE confirm token / ONE transaction (atomic, rollbackтЖТre-proposable), collapsing the fragile propose-kindтЖТapproveтЖТpropose-each-attrтЖТapprove chain. +2 real-PG tests. **LIVE-PROVEN:** natural request тЖТ agent made ONE propose_new_kind call WITH attributes (powers/coven, each described) тЖТ ONE approve тЖТ `witch` kind + both attrs (with descriptions) landed atomically. F3c stay-on-task guard kills the web-search/list-templates tangent.
>   - **тЬЕ F1 `D-GLOSSARY-SYSTEM-ATTR-DESCRIPTIONS` тАФ BUILT (2026-06-22).** Spec [`docs/specs/2026-06-22-F1-system-attr-descriptions.md`](../specs/2026-06-22-F1-system-attr-descriptions.md). New migration **0035_system_attr_descriptions** ([system_attr_descriptions.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/system_attr_descriptions.go), registered in [ledger.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/ledger.go)): an idempotent, non-clobbering `UPDATE system_attributes` over the spec's 81 authored `(kind_code, attr_code)` cells (name/term display keys omitted) that sets `description` + recomputes `content_hash` with the EXACT inline md5 the seed/`attrContentHash` uses тАФ so the new hash equals a fresh seed-with-description (the parity adopt + G5 Sync depend on). `COALESCE(TRIM(description),'')=''` guard тЗТ admin edits survive + re-run = 0 rows. **Decision honored: sync-only, NO backfill** тАФ new books carry descriptions at adopt; existing books pull via `sync_apply take_theirs` (`keep_mine` preserves a local edit). Authored table re-verified against `domain.DefaultKinds` (exact 81/93). **LIVE-SMOKE (real dev DB `loreweave_glossary`, rebuilt+restarted glossary-service тЖТ RunChain applied 0035 on actual startup):** `schema_migrations` now has `0035`; `system_attributes` 0тЖТ**81** descriptions (name/term still 0); `character.aliases` hash moved `0be5469aтАж`тЖТ`5ae3679eтАж`; and **22,736 existing system-sourced book_attributes now drift** (`source_hash тЙа` updated System `content_hash`) across 303 books тАФ the exact surface `glossary_book_sync_available` lists, so existing books pull via Sync. **End-to-end on a REAL book via the gateway:** on `019ee969` (ф╕ЗхПдчеЮх╕Э) the extraction-profile started at **0/60** descriptions; `GET тАж/sync/available` listed **51** attribute updates; `POST тАж/sync/apply take_theirs` applied all 51; the profile then read **51/60** with `character.aliases: Other names, titles, epithetsтАж` тАФ the authored text now flowing through the worker's cross-service extraction-profile. VERIFY (real PG 5555, throwaway DBs): migrate-pkg mechanics test (93 emptyтЖТ81 set + 12 name/term untouched; hash moves + equals seed-formula; idempotent; non-clobber) + api-pkg propagation test (adopt-before-edit тЖТ `sync/available` lists attrs with authored `theirs` тЖТ take_theirs pulls / keep_mine preserves; new-adopt carries descriptions immediately; idempotent count=2 on shared DB) тАФ both green; full glossary module `-p1` green (api/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt + vet + ai-provider-gate clean. **M3 extraction-prompt smoke not run** (the adoptтЖТ`book_attributes.description` landing тАФ exactly what `extraction_prompt.py` reads тАФ is proven by the api test; a full worker e2e rides `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE`).

Earlier this branch: S1/S4a/S5/S6/S7/S9/S10/S11/S13/S20/S26. **S8 token-truncation bug тАФ `D-EXTRACTION-TOKEN-TRUNCATION`, FIXED + live-re-verified (don't blame the model):** the S8 "0 entities" was NOT a weak model тАФ direct LM Studio replay proved **output-token truncation**: `plan_kind_batches` budgeted only the input schema, packing **7 kinds into one call** whose output hit `finish_reason=length` at `max_tokens=12000` тЖТ cut mid-array тЖТ 0 parseable entities. Fix: cap `MAX_KINDS_PER_BATCH=3` (root cause тАФ bounds per-call output), raise `max_tokens` 12000тЖТ20000, and repair truncated/fenced arrays instead of discarding them ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py) + [extraction_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_worker.py)); +5 regression tests. **Live:** same chapter now extracts 9 created + 5 skipped across 3 clean `finish=stop` batches (glossary 10тЖТ19). Book-native rows carry `source_ref=NULL`; attribute create enforces the **book-local FK guard** (kind_id/genre_id must be live rows of THIS book тЖТ 422, blocks a cross-book ref the raw FK would accept). Contract extended (`book-ontology` tag, 11 paths incl. retroactive adopt/ontology, 11 schemas, `Forbidden` resp). Generic spec-driven PATCH plumbing (`scanPatchFields`/`applyBookUpdate`, table/col are constants тЖТ no SQLi). **VERIFY:** 3 new api tests green тАФ FullLifecycle (create/patch/delete all 3 levels + active replace-set + kind-genres row + cascade-on-delete asserts), NonOwnerForbidden (403 on every write), **EditCollaboratorDeniedManage** (an *edit* grantee reads /ontology=200 but every write=403 тЖТ proves the gate is Manage, not mere auth); full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` green (22.8s); gofmt/vet/build/provider-gate clean. Single-service change (glossary + contracts) тЖТ no cross-service live-smoke token needed. /review-impl-grade self-review folded a `requireBookManage` extraction (killed 7 `_=userID` + 4 duplicated preambles).
> - **ЁЯФи D-GKA-ADOPT-USER-TIER BUILT (this session, caller-scoped тАФ PO chose (a)).** Adopt now merges **System тЖТ the adopting CALLER's User tier**: for each picked code, the caller's user-tier row shadows the System one (user rows inserted FIRST, System with `ON CONFLICT (book_id,code) DO NOTHING` fills the rest). All 3 levels тАФ genre/kind/attribute (+ kindтЖФgenre links unioned). No new contract (ownerтЙИcaller under the Manage gate; the person setting up uses their own customizations тАФ arguably more correct than strict owner-scoping). `source_ref='user:<id>'` distinguishes the provenance; user genres/attrs carry their `content_hash`, user kinds get an inline md5 (no content_hash col); `is_hidden=NOT is_active`, `sort_order=0` (user_kinds has no sort_order). VERIFY: new `TestBookAdopt_UserTierShadowsSystem` (xianxia+universal genres + character kind + a 'name' attr all user-customized тЖТ user-sourced; romance not customized тЖТ system-sourced) + existing adopt/CRUD suites green; full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` 22.9s; gofmt/provider-gate clean.
> - **ЁЯФи G4 тАФ DESTRUCTIVE CUTOVER DONE (this session, user-approved big-bang; built via a 9-agent Workflow + human foundation/verify).** The entity layer now lives on the BOOK tier. **Migration** (`migrate.go`): `UpGlossaryCutoverG4` (TRUNCATE entities CASCADE; repoint `glossary_entities.kind_id`тЖТ`book_kinds`, `entity_attribute_values.attr_def_id`тЖТ`book_attributes`; rewrite `recalculate_entity_snapshot` to book tier, source `'book'`) + `UpMergeCandidatesG4` (merge_candidates.kind_idтЖТbook_kinds) + `UpGlossaryCutoverG4Cache` (restore cached_name/search_vector maintenance on the book-tier snapshot) + `UpGlossaryDropLegacyG4` (DROP `genre_groups`, `system_kind_attributes`, `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`.`genre_tags`). Wired LAST in main.go + the test chains. **Seed rewrite:** `SeedGenreKindAttr` now derives system_genres/kind_genres/attributes from `domain.DefaultKinds` in Go (no longer reads the dropped table/column). **Handlers retargeted to book-local:** extraction (loadKindMap/loadAttrDefMap[universal-genre DISTINCT ON]/createExtractedEntity/mergeExtractedEntity/findEntityByNameOrAlias/getKnownEntities/writeExtractionProfile[genre_groupsтЖТbook_active_genres]), entity detail, glossary_translate, internalTranslationGlossary, listKinds (тЖТsystem_kind_genres/system_attributes), and **O2** тАФ `confirmSchema`/`createKindFromParams`/`createAttrDefFromParams` now mint into the **BOOK** tier (token-bound bookID, book-scoped, `errNotAdopted`тЖТ422; a user's assistant can no longer mutate the shared catalogue). Legacy per-book `genre_groups` routes/handlers RETIRED (`genres_crud.go`/`genres_handler.go`/`domain/genres.go` deleted). **E3 KG clear:** `reset_glossary_anchors` added to knowledge-service (manual maintenance fn, not auto-run). **Precondition:** a book MUST be adopted before entities can be created in it. **VERIFY (human-run, not trusted from agents):** full `go test ./... -p 1 -count=1` GREEN across api(53s)/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc; `go build`+`go vet` clean; no production request-path reader of the dropped objects remains (grep-verified тАФ all refs are migration DDL/seed/comments/DefaultKinds struct); provider-gate OK; gofmt clean; knowledge entities.py compiles. **Dev-DB cutover NOT run** тАФ it's a deploy step; only the throwaway `loreweave_glossary_test` was migrated.
> - **ЁЯФм /review-impl on G4 (this session) тАФ found + fixed a CRITICAL data-loss bug.** The cutover's `TRUNCATE glossary_entities CASCADE` was UNGUARDED; migrations re-run every service boot (execGuarded has no ledger), so every production restart after cutover would have wiped ALL entities + wiki. Tests never surfaced it (they want a clean slate). FIXED: the TRUNCATE now runs only while the OLD `glossary_entities_kind_id_fkey` (тЖТsystem_kinds) still exists тАФ i.e. exactly once per DB, pre-cutover. Regression: `TestGlossaryCutoverG4_GuardPreventsRepeatTruncate` (migrate pkg, ephemeral DB: cutover тЖТ seed entity тЖТ re-run cutover тЖТ entity SURVIVES + FK repointed). Other findings accept+documented: revision-restore would break on a pre-cutover snapshot (impossible under full-reset тАФ cutover cascades entity_revisions); extraction on an un-adopted book silently skips all entities (`D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD`, LOW). Writer audit otherwise clean (createEntity was the only miss, fixed in 222786b4).
> - **ЁЯФи G5 тАФ book SYNC (diff/apply) DONE (this session, + D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH resolved).** `book_sync_handler.go`: `GET /v1/glossary/books/{id}/sync/available` (View-gated diff тАФ compares each adopted book row's frozen `source_hash` vs the live source's current content hash: `update_available` when divergent, `source_retired` when the source was deleted/purged; up-to-date rows omitted) + `POST тАж/sync/apply` (Manage-gated, per-row `keep_mine`|`take_theirs`; `take_theirs` overwrites the SEMANTIC/hash surface [genreтЖТname; kindтЖТname+description; attributeтЖТname+description+field_type+is_required+options] + refreshes `source_hash`; `keep_mine` bumps `source_hash` only; validate-all-before-mutate + same per-book advisory lock as adopt; cross-book id тЖТ 0 rows, no leak). **D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH fixed**: `createUserGenre`/`patchUserGenre`/`patchUserAttribute` now (re)compute `content_hash` (was never set on genres / never refreshed on patch тЖТ sync was blind to user-standard edits). Contract: `sync` tag + 2 paths + 5 schemas. **VERIFY:** full `go test ./... -p 1` GREEN (api 42s + 5 new G5 tests: diff/apply all-choices [kind+attr take_theirs full-surface, genre keep_mine], source-retired, user-edit-refreshes-hash end-to-end, patchUserAttribute hash, View/Manage grant gates; events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt/vet/provider-gate clean; contract YAML valid. **/review-impl: 2 MED coverage gaps fixed in-loop** (kind take_theirs + attr full-surface were unasserted; patchUserAttribute hash untested) + invariant doc at `attrContentHash`. Single-service (glossary + contracts) тЖТ no cross-service token needed.
> - **ЁЯФм /review-impl ALSO found + FIXED a pre-existing G4 PROD TIME-BOMB (this session).** The migration chain leaked a `system_kinds` `pg_attribute` slot **per run**: base `Up` did `ALTER TABLE system_kinds ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS genre_tags` (re-add) while G4e `DROP COLUMN genre_tags` removed it тАФ Postgres never reclaims dropped-column slots without a table rewrite, so each service boot leaked one slot toward the 1600-column ceiling (after which the service can't start). It had already exhausted the shared test DB (1590 dropped slots тЖТ blocked the events+migrate test packages). **FIX (`migrate.go`):** `genre_tags` FULLY RETIRED from the additive chain тАФ removed from `system_kinds` CREATE + the re-add ALTER + the `unknown`-kind seed + `Seed()` insert, and from `user_kinds` CREATE; G4e's `DROP COLUMN тАж IF EXISTS` kept as a ONE-TIME legacy cleanup (no longer fights a re-add). `genre_tags` was write-only mid-chain (only `Seed` wrote it; `SeedGenreKindAttr` derives `system_kind_genres` from `DefaultKinds` in Go, never read the column; handler `genre_tags` are output aliases from `system_kind_genres`). **PROVEN:** repeated full-suite migration runs now hold the dropped-slot count steady at 1590 (no growth) and the full suite is green; a FRESH DB from the fixed code carries 0 dead slots.

The collapse is `createEntity`'s value-seeding `SELECT DISTINCT ON (ba.code) тАж WHERE ba.kind_id=$1` тАФ it keeps ONE attr per code (universal-preferred), so a keep-both conflict gets one value row. Fix = seed one row per (kind,genre,code) [drop the DISTINCT-ON-code collapse] + verify the snapshot read tolerates 2 same-code rows (~50 LOC, careful, NO migration). **Still undone (gated/large, treat as not-done):** the live `loreweave_glossary` DB cutover (G4 ran on test DB only); `D-GKA-G4-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-GKA-G6F-PLAYWRIGHT-SMOKE` (stack down); `D-GKA-FE-STANDARDS-LIBRARY` (~850 LOC, 4 sub-milestones тАФ User-tier UI; needs UX placement decision); `D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT` (~350 LOC тАФ admin write surface for System standards; blocked on the platform admin-identity epic, can ship behind a placeholder `requireAdmin`); `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP`/`D-GKA-SYSTEM-KINDS-RECLAIM` (migrate.go, optional/deploy-time, marginal тАФ gated). `D-I18N-PREEXISTING-DRIFT` (en-only world/knowledge/composition/books.settings.world keys тАФ unrelated to G, fails `i18n:check`).

> - **тЬЕ SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN + ADMIN CMS DONE + LIVE-SMOKED (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`, 2026-06-20; commits `3d1763e3` Lane A, `f90cb1fc` Lane B+C; pushed).** The admin write surface for the platform System tier + a separate thin CMS to drive it. Built fan-out (BE myself for the auth boundary; 2 background agents scaffolded+wired the CMS). **Lane A тАФ glossary admin API (`3d1763e3`):** `requireAdminScope` verifies an RS256 admin JWT via shared `contracts/adminjwt` (`ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`, base64-or-PEM), scope `admin:write`; POST/PATCH/DELETE for `system-genres`/`system-kinds`/`system-attributes-admin` with `content_hash` recompute (clears `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`); `universal`/`unknown` guarded un-deletable. A valid HS256 USER token is rejected (RS256-only) тЖТ a regular user can never mutate System tier. Unset key тЖТ 503 fail-closed. Test: `TestSystemTierAdmin_*` (CRUD, 401 user-token, 403 scope, 503 unconfigured, hash recompute). **Lane B тАФ auth admin-session exchange (`f90cb1fc`):** `POST /v1/admin/session` тАФ a logged-in user who is an ACTIVE `admin_principal` SELF-mints an RS256 admin JWT (subject from their own token, authority only from the DB row; non-admin 403, disabled 404, rate-limited, audited). New `authjwt.LocalKeySigner` = production-safe in-process RSA signer for DEV/self-hosted (no KMS); `main.go` selects KMSтЖТlocalтЖТdisabled; `AdminIssuanceEnabled` now true for either path. Gateway proxies `/v1/admin`тЖТauth. Tests `TestAdminSession_*`. **Lane C тАФ `cms-frontend/` (NEW, separate from `frontend/`):** Vite+React+TS+Tailwind thin admin UI on :5175тЖТgateway `/v1`; login does the admin-session exchange + stores the admin token; Genres/Kinds/Attributes CRUD panels wired to the admin endpoints (13 vitest; tsc+build green). **infra:** compose passes admin keys through (disabled by defaultтЖТfail-closed) + a `cms-frontend` service; `gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` mints a dev keypair; auth+glossary Dockerfiles now `COPY contracts/adminjwt`. **LIVE-SMOKED** (`admin-cms-live-smoke.ps1`, full stack, dev local-RSA тАФ auth signs + glossary verifies, **kid matched** `bd90034eтАж`): login тЖТ `/v1/admin/session` (role=admin) тЖТ System genre CREATE/PATCH/DELETE through the gateway; plain USER token тЖТ 401; non-admin тЖТ 403. **/review-impl (auth boundary, no HIGH/MED):** user-token bypass impossible (RS256 vs HS256, proven), no self-escalation (authority from `admin_principals` only), scope-gated, key-identity-pinned (kid/iss/aud/exp), fail-closed everywhere. LOW/accept: adminSession doesn't check session-revocation (consistent with every access-token endpoint), break_glass not distinguished (pre-existing R3), admin token in CMS localStorage (15m TTL, re-exchanged on 401). **For a persistent dev CMS:** run `infra/gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` once, export the two vars, `docker compose up -d --build auth-service glossary-service api-gateway-bff cms-frontend`, then seed an `admin_principals` row for your user. **Remaining GKA debt:** `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` тАФ now CLEARED by the migration-ledger bullet below.
> - **тЦ╢ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE тАФ Glossary Assistant: Tiered MCP Tool Expansion (specced + planned + ambiguity-resolved; NOT built).** The agent's MCP surface only covers the pre-tiering world (entities + flat schema); the tiered model (System/User/Book ontology, adopt, sync, user standards) + admin System tier are HTTP-only. ~16 new tools across 5 phases. **Three design artifacts are DONE this session** (all committed, see below): the **spec** [`тАжtiered-tools.md`](../specs/2026-06-20-glossary-assistant-tiered-tools.md) (┬з11 edge-case register from a 13-case stress-test; ┬з5.1 confirm-token contract; ┬з12 resolves 8 deep-dive ambiguities against real code) + the **parallel build plan** [`тАжtiered-tools-buildplan.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-glossary-assistant-tiered-tools-buildplan.md) (DAG with compose points CP-0..CP-7 = sync barriers, async streams between them). **тЦ╢ FIRST ACTION next session: CP-0 (confirm contract, ~1 design pass) тЖТ FOUNDATION (`/loom`, human-in-loop, `/review-impl` on F1).** Foundation = F1 generalized confirm-token machinery (glossary `action_confirm.go` + `consumed_tokens` as a NEW ledger chain entry `0030_*` тАФ go through RunChain!) тИе F2 shared helpers (codeтЖТid resolvers, base-version 409, read-tool rename `list_kinds`тЖТ`list_system_standards` + new `book_ontology_read`) тИе F3 reusable FE confirm-card. Then T1 book тЖТ CP-2 тЖТ T2 sync тИе T3 user тЖТ CP-3 тЖТ CP-4 admin contract тЖТ T4a glossary/admin тИе T4b gateway/admin тЖТ T4c chat AdminContext тЖТ T4d cms chat panel (long pole, own mini-design) тЖТ T5. **Locked decisions:** admin MCP is a PHYSICALLY SEPARATE `/mcp/admin` endpoint, transport-gated by RS256 before `tools/list`, with the gateway exposing a separate `/mcp/admin` downstream too (INV-T6 end-to-end тАФ gateway federates by tool-name into one catalog, so admin names would leak otherwise); admin authority = RS256 `X-Admin-Token` never `X-User-Id`; every System write human-confirm-gated; confirm-tokens single-use (`consumed_tokens`) + re-validated at confirm; patch tools carry a base version (content_hash / updated_at) 409-on-drift; adopt = class C; 3 verbs ├Ч `level` arg; per-tier tools register in their OWN Go file (`RegisterXTools`) to enable parallel streams. Two phase-level opens (not blockers): T4d CMS chat-panel mini-design (port main-FE SSE infra), `admin:read` scope (lean reuse `admin:write`). **Unpushed commits this session:** `acce1820` (spec+handoff), `2831ee20` (ambiguity resolutions), `023ad55e` (build plan) тАФ on top of pushed `eb8536e1` (migration ledger).
> - **тЬЕ MIGRATION LEDGER DONE тАФ `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` CLEARED (2026-06-20, this session; L; plan [`тАжmigration-ledger.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-glossary-migration-ledger.md); NOT pushed).** Root-fixed the create-then-drop churn properly: the glossary chain was LEDGER-FREE (every idempotent step replayed each boot тАФ most visibly CREATE+seedтЖТDROP of `system_kind_attributes` per boot; harmless recycle but pure churn, and entangled with the extraction_audit_log FK + the pre-cutover snapshot fn's JOINs so it couldn't be removed in isolation). New `internal/migrate/ledger.go` (`schema_migrations` table + `EnsureLedger`/`ApplyOnce`/`RunChain` over an ordered 29-step `chain` `0001_schemaтАж0029_glossary_drop_legacy_g4`) makes each step run **exactly once**; `main.go`'s ~155-line unconditional sequence collapses to one `migrate.RunChain`. **Purely additive тАФ ZERO migration SQL changed**; every step keeps its own internal guard (the cutover's FK-existence TRUNCATE guard intact as defense-in-depth independent of the ledger). The 2 async background backfills stay OUT of the chain (non-blocking, self-limiting). **Behavior change (documented loudly in code):** seeds are now ledgered тЖТ new `DefaultKinds` seed data needs a NEW chain entry, not auto-pickup on deploy. **VERIFY:** `go build`/`vet`/`gofmt` clean; **6 migrate tests** green (3 new: freshтЖТno-churn, idempotent├Ч3, apply-once incl. failed-step-NOT-recorded; + the new **`TestRunChain_TransitionPreservesData`**; + 2 pre-existing rename/seed); api test pkg compiles. **LIVE transition smoke on the REAL un-ledgered dev DB** (`loreweave_glossary` тАФ already cut over, no ledger): clean boot тЖТ **29 ledger rows**, `system_kind_attributes` dropped, **2 entities / 13 system_kinds / 10 book_kinds PRESERVED** (cutover TRUNCATE did not re-fire); 2nd restart idempotent + healthy + ledger stable. **/review-impl: 1 MED + 1 LOW FIXED in-loop** тАФ MED: `EnsureLedger`'s bare `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` ran OUTSIDE the migration advisory lock (concurrent-startup race тЖТ possible `os.Exit(1)` on one replica; Postgres CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS isn't concurrency-safe) тЖТ now routed through `execGuarded`; LOW: added the repeatable transition/data-loss regression test (was live-smoke-only). Accept+documented: seed-semantics footgun; no enforcement of append-only chain ordering (standard migration discipline, noted in the `chain` doc). Single-service (glossary only) тЖТ no cross-service token needed.

New `neo4j_helpers.purge_project` (project_id-scoped `DETACH DELETE` + drops only this project's summary indexes, NOT the shared dimension ones) wired best-effort into `delete_project` after the owner-gated Postgres delete. Tests: neo4j_helpers 31 + public_projects 87. **тЬЕ LIVE-SMOKED + SWEPT (2026-06-18, image rebuilt + pushed `3dfb2199`):** endpoint smoke (create `general` project тЖТ inject 3 nodes + a summary index тЖТ `DELETE` тЖТ 204; Neo4j 3тЖТ0, index dropped, shared indexes survived, PG row 0, log `purged neo4j тАж 3 nodes, 1 indexes`); then swept the EXISTING orphans тАФ **106 graphs / 2342 nodes** (not ~7; only 11 of 108 graphs had a live PG row) DETACH-DELETE'd by the live-set predicate + the 1 orphan's 3 summary indexes dropped, 2 live graphs + 9 shared indexes intact. Audit: `docs/raid/evidence/neo4j-orphan-sweep/2026-06-18-orphan-audit.txt`. **Net: found a real bug, fixed it, live-proved it, AND reclaimed 2342 orphaned nodes тАФ net debt shrinks.** Fresh throwaway 1-ch campaign run (createdтЖТstartedтЖТcancelledтЖТfully cleaned up, 0 residue): **S3b backoff** (knowledge `benchmark_missing` 409 тЖТ saga re-dispatched at exact 5s intervals, attempts=3) + **cancel** (runningтЖТcancelling). **Remaining ~7 need a benchmark-FIXTURED extraction run (Neo4j build+cleanup) or are non-deterministic/browser:** cancel-prop-to-a-running-child (this run 409'd pre-job so `knowledge_job_id` was NULL тАФ nothing to propagate; needs the K17.9 fixture), rerank-byok, S2 idempotency, S3a governor, S5a estimate (NULL тАФ confirm not-recorded-vs-gap), breaker-pause (non-deterministic), S5c+S6 (browser). The saga demonstrably completes E2E (multiple `completed` campaigns exist).
> - **тЬЕ `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION` SHIPPED (2026-06-17, /loom L + /review-impl, design [`тАжcomposition-retry-design.md`](../specs/2026-06-17-composition-retry-design.md)).** The earlier "bifurcated тЖТ not clean" triage was REFINED: retryability is **per-job** (worker-drivable тЯ║ reconstructable), not per-kind (composition `kind` = the free-form `operation`, identical for the worker + inline paths). **Design realized with NO migration** via the existing `job_projection.params` JSONB: composition `create` emits `params.retryable = is_worker_drivable(operation, input)` (a job is worker-drivable iff the server stamped a recognized `input.worker_op`); jobs-service `derive_control_caps(status, kind, *, retryable=None)` offers RETRY on failed when `kind тИИ _RETRYABLE_KINDS OR retryable is True`; both read sites (`routers/jobs.py` `_with_caps` + `control_job` gate + `sse.py`) pass the flag. composition `internal_job_control` routes `retry` тЖТ `_retry_generation_job_core`: 404-not-owned тЖТ 409-unless-failed тЖТ 409-not-worker-drivable (re-checked on the row) тЖТ 409-worker-disabled тЖТ re-submit a NEW pending job (copies `input`, `idempotency_key=None`, **guarded create for BOTH chapter-draft writers `chapter_generate`+`stitch_chapter`**) тЖТ enqueue. **/review-impl: 2 MED fixed** тАФ (1) drivability was over-broad via an `operation` fallback (inline stitch / user `operation="generate"` falsely retryable тЖТ instant re-fail); tightened to the server-set `worker_op` only + stamped `worker_op` on the stitch (engine.py) + decompose (plan.py) worker inputs; (2) stitch retry bypassed the chapter in-flight guard тЖТ now guarded. FE/SDK ZERO change (Retry data-driven off `control_caps`). VERIFY: jobs-service 29 (caps incl. TestClient read-API wiring) ┬╖ composition 95 (retry/emit/worker/plan/engine). **Retry now complete for translation + extraction + video_gen + composition.** Deferred: `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-SSE-CAPS` (LOW тАФ a freshly-failed composition job's LIVE SSE frame omits the retry cap since the failed emit carries `params=None`; read API authoritative, refetch/reconnect recovers it) + `D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-COMPOSITION-LIVE-SMOKE` (needs worker stack + a deterministically-failing composition job). Lore stays the only un-wired retry (`D-JOBS-P4-RETRY-LORE` тАФ sync in-process тЖТ async-decouple refactor).

Key finding: a decoupled job submits ONE chunk/poll then waits ([runner.py:1948](../../services/worker-ai/app/runner.py#L1948)), so the per-owner cap only bites across CONCURRENT jobs тАФ what the smoke had to create. **(6) `D-P5-M2` (multi-owner)** тАФ with owner1 at cap, seeded 2 running jobs for a 2nd owner (own BYOK + book; rows direct-inserted as running тАФ worker poll/acquire path is identical): `inflight:{owner1}=1` + `inflight:{owner2}=1` + `total=2` simultaneously тЖТ owner2 acquired its OWN slot despite owner1 saturated, owner2's 2nd job independently deferred. Per-owner ZSET key makes isolation structural. **(7) `D-JOBS-P3-KNOWLEDGE-CANCEL-SUCCESS`** тАФ cancelled the inflight job A via the public cancel endpoint тЖТ 200 cancelled; `extraction_jobs`тЖТcancelled; `job.cancelled` outbox (H1 same-tx) тЖТ relay тЖТ `job_projection = knowledge|extraction|cancelled`. All synthetic data cleaned (4 jobs/projects, 2 benchmarks, projection rows, 151 Neo4j nodes); `P5_OWNER_CAP` restored to 5.
>   - **тЬЕ `D-JOBS-SECONDARY-KIND-CONTROL` CLEARED (2026-06-17, this commit)** тАФ unified control now works for the secondary kinds. `kind` rides the control forward body (`forward_control`+`jobs.py`; other services ignore it тАФ Pydantic `extra='ignore'`, verified no `forbid` anywhere). `derive_control_caps` gives each its NATIVE caps: glossary_extraction/glossary_translation = cancel-only (pending|running); wiki_gen = cancel(pending|paused)+resume(paused), NO running-cancel (D-WIKI-M7B); `book_import` stays view-only (no control endpoint). translation `control_job` тЖТ `_cancel_secondary_core` (owner-scoped, 404/409, UPDATEтЖТcancelling+emit on extraction_jobs/glossary_translation_jobs by a fixed kindтЖТtable map); knowledge `control_extraction_job` тЖТ `_control_wiki_gen_job` (owner re-check via repo.get, repo.cancel/resume which emit). No FE change (caps flow through the SSE event; JobControls already renders per-cap). **/review-impl: 1 HIGH fixed** тАФ the unified wiki resume only flipped pausedтЖТpending but did NOT re-enqueue (the consumer is event-driven тЖТ job stuck pending until a process-restart drain); fixed to mirror the native `resume_wiki_gen_job` (`enqueue_wiki_gen(_redis(), job_id)` after `repo.resume`), +2 tests (resume re-enqueues, cancel doesn't). Verified no other native side-effect omissions (cancel=repo.cancel both; glossary cancel=same cancelling+emit flow); owner-scoping is consistent with the unified plane's existing owner-only contract. Verify: jobs-service 70 ┬╖ translation 780 ┬╖ knowledge 2506 ┬╖ provider-gate OK. Live-smoke тЖТ `D-SECONDARY-KIND-CONTROL-LIVE-SMOKE`.

M2.1 тЬЕ + M2.2 тЬЕ; M3 тП│ NEXT.** Plan [`docs/plans/2026-06-15-translation-panel-t2-m2-segment-translate.md`](../plans/2026-06-15-translation-panel-t2-m2-segment-translate.md). Split at a risk boundary into **M2.1 (status foundation)** тЬЕ committed `45fffdf5` + **M2.2 (dirty-only re-translate)** тЬЕ.
> - **тЬЕ M2.1 SHIPPED (translation-service only).** `segment_translations` table (per chapter/lang/segment; `source_content_hash` at translate-time, UNIQUE(chapter,lang,seg) + idx); [`workers/segment_status.py`](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/segment_status.py) `record_segment_translations` (full-chapter translate upserts all segments) + `compute_segment_status` (current vs recorded тЖТ `dirty`/`translated`); finalize hook `_record_segment_status` (best-effort post-commit; records against EXISTING segments, only fetches+builds if none тАФ no hot-path book-service call); internal + public `GET тАж/chapters/{id}/segments/status?target_language=` (public = book-VIEW, leak-safe). **VERIFY:** 717 pytest (+ real-PG recordтЖТeditтЖТdirtyтЖТre-record cycle; DDL applied to dev PG :5555). **/review-impl:** MED-1 (per-chapter book-service fetch + finalize-time rebuild would falsely mark a mid-translation edit as translated тЖТ record-existing-first, fetch only if absent) + MED-2 (real best-effort swallow untested тЖТ +3 hook tests) FIXED.
>   - **Dirty semantics (doc):** `dirty` = the segment's SOURCE changed since last translate (or never translated for this lang). NOT glossary-staleness (that's `is_glossary_stale`, M3) and NOT translation-quality (a fallback-to-original block still reads "translated").
> - **тЬЕ M2.2 SHIPPED (translation-service only).** Dirty-only re-translate via the existing job machinery. `CreateJobPayload`+`translation_jobs`+job/coordinator message gain `block_index_filter`+`seed_version_id` (additive migration). Worker `_process_chapter` block path: a partial branch (`_partial_retranslate_blocks`) translates ONLY the dirty blocks and overlays them onto the seed version's body (unchanged blocks copied verbatim), finalizing a normal new llm version тАФ **human-guard free** via `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL`. New `POST /v1/translation/chapters/{id}/retranslate-dirty` (book EDIT): dirty segments тЖТ block range, seed = latest llm version, enqueues a single-chapter `force_retranslate` job. **Guards:** seed must align 1:1 with current source else full-re-translate fallback; all-OOR filter тЖТ seed copy, no LLM spend; total-failure guard preserved. **VERIFY:** 727 pytest (overlay incl. dup/unsorted/mismatch/empty; endpoint dirty-union + 409 no-dirty/no-seed; seed chapter-scope; `_block_plain_text`); provider-gate clean; DDL applied to dev PG :5555. **/review-impl: HIGH-1** (seed loaded with no chapter scoping тЖТ cross-chapter seed IDOR via public create-job fields тЖТ `_load_seed_blocks` now `WHERE id=$1 AND chapter_id=$2`) **FIXED**; **+3 user-requested hardenings:** public `create_job` strips `block_index_filter`/`seed_version_id` (endpoint-only), partial path honors the pipeline's v3 verifier (injected `translate_blocks_fn`, not hardcoded v2), full-chapter memo from the merged body. **тЪая╕П `segment_index`/block positions assume stable structure** тАФ overlay falls back to full re-translate on any count mismatch; orphan `segment_translations` rows (segment-count shrink) are invisible to `compute` but a cleanup is owed.
> - **ЁЯФи M3 IN PROGRESS (FULL A+B+C+D, plan [`...t2-m3-segment-matrix.md`](../plans/2026-06-15-translation-panel-t2-m3-segment-matrix.md)).** Risk-boundary commits: **M3.1 (A coverage) тЬЕ** + **M3.2 (D per-segment glossary staleness) тП│** + **M3.3 (B drill-down FE + C limit fix) тП│**.
>   - **тЬЕ M3.1:** `GET /v1/translation/books/{id}/segment-coverage?target_language=` (book VIEW) тЖТ per-chapter `{segment_total, translated_count, dirty_count, stale_count(0 till M3.2), needs_count}`. Lang-scoped book_chapters тЛИ chapter_segments тЛИ segment_translations. VERIFY: 731 pytest (+ real-PG count test: 3 segs/1 dirty, untranslatedтЖТall dirty, foreign-langтЖТempty).
>   - **тЬЕ M3.2 (D):** `segment_glossary_usage` table (per-segment entity refs, language-independent; populated best-effort at finalize by scanning segment_text vs the book glossary source terms тАФ `_record_segment_glossary_usage`) + `segment_translations.is_glossary_stale` (reset false on re-record) + glossary_consumer per-segment flag (precise entity path joins segment_glossary_usage; coarse path stays chapter-level) + `stale`/`needs` in segment status + `stale_count`/`needs_count` in coverage + retranslate-dirty acts on `needs` (dirty тИк stale). VERIFY: 735 pytest (+ real-PG glossary-staleness cycle: usageтЖТentity_updated flags only the using segmentтЖТstatus staleтЖТre-record clears; scan_glossary_usage pure). **Known LOW:** finalize re-fetches the glossary + scans per segment (perf-deferrable); usage scan capped at the endpoint's top-200 entities; substring term match mirrors build_glossary_context. (/review-impl follow-up: the entity set is language-INDEPENDENT тАФ the endpoint LEFT-JOINs the target translation тАФ so no last-language-wins drift; usage fetch bumped to max_entries=200 + INSERTs batched.)

VERIFY: tsc 0; vitest 48 translation/book-tabs (+3 segmentDrilldown). Deferred `D-TRANSL-T2M3-LIVE-SMOKE` (matrixтЖТbadgeтЖТdrill-downтЖТre-translate-changed on the stack; v3-book partial verifier).

Two deferred big-features designed + design-reviewed twice (build deferred by user). **Anchor = `chapter_blocks` (block-range), NOT `scenes`** тАФ scenes are an import-decomposer artifact absent on the user's bulk-`.txt` corpus (and on legacy); `chapter_blocks` is trigger-extracted for every chapter. > **ЁЯФи SHARED `<ChapterListBrowser>` (B1) тАФ foundation DONE; full 7-site migration is an OVER-FIT (2026-06-14, pushed).** RAID-unblock re-checked: C22тЖТC28 are all composition/creation-flywheel; no remaining cycle touches a chapter-list call-site; `features/composition/*` isn't a call-site; `ChapterEditorPage` has no remaining brief + no uncommitted edits тЖТ **all call-sites RAID-free.**
> - **Shipped:** BE `editorial_status` (draft/published+pubrev gate) + `q` (title/filename ILIKE, 256-cap) on public `GET /v1/books/{id}/chapters` (`c963e466`, live-smoked: published 5 / draft 0 / bogus 400 / q='чмм1' 1). `<ChapterListBrowser>` (`frontend/src/components/shared/`) тАФ server-paged (useServerPagedList+Pager), modes none/single/multi, debounced search, selection Set persists across pages + "select all N matching" loop-fetch, filters server-side. tsc 0 + vitest 5. **First consumer:** ExtractionWizard **pick** mode (`116470cf`) тАФ fixes the pick-cap (couldn't select past the first page).
> - **Per-site fit analysis (why NOT a clean 7-site swap тАФ the recorded B1 over-fit lesson, confirmed per-site):**
>   - `ChaptersTab` тАФ **already paginated** (offset + `<Pagination>`) with a **richer DataTable** (language/status/updated/actions). Migrating = downgrade тЖТ **skip.**
>   - `ReaderPage` TOC + `ChapterEditorPage` nav тАФ need the **full ordered chapter list in memory** for prev/next + reading-progress, not a paginated UI. Their cap fix is "loop-fetch all", NOT this browser. **Misfit.**
>   - `TranslationTab` тАФ chapters├Чlanguages **matrix** (2D grid), not a flat list. **Misfit.**
>   - `ContextPicker` тАФ **multi-book** picker; single-book browser doesn't fit directly. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below).
>   - `ChapterRangeStep` (campaign) тАФ range-by-`sort_order` predicate (from/to numeric inputs + a count), not a list browser. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below). (StepBatchConfig **all/range** were fixed in /review-impl тАФ see below.)
>   - **тЬЕ `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-RANGE-MODE` + `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-CONTEXTPICKER` CLEARED (2026-06-14, won't-fit + real-bug-fixed).** Per-site analysis confirmed neither is a `<ChapterListBrowser>` fit, so the "reusable range mode" / "per-book browser" component work is dropped as over-fit (the B1 anti-pattern). The one genuine harm behind RANGE-MODE тАФ campaign `ChapterRangeStep` fetched `limit:5000`тЖТclamped 100, so the displayed "N published / N selected" **count under-reported** on >100-ch books (the campaign scope itself is a server-side `chapter_from/to` predicate, never the fetched list тЖТ no scope bug) тАФ was fixed **in place** with a loop-fetch (no new component). `ContextPicker` requests `limit:100` per book (at the clamp, no silent-20 bug); first-100/book is acceptable for a chat context picker and a per-book browser is the wrong shape (it's a multi-book grouped picker) тЖТ won't-fix.
> - **/review-impl fixes (this turn):** **#1/#2** ChapterListBrowser now resets page on a filter-prop change (render-time guard, not an effect) + shows a jump-to-first on an out-of-range page (was a false "No chapters"). **#3** StepBatchConfig now **loop-fetches the full chapter list** so `all`/`range` no longer silently truncate to 100 on big books (the default-select-all bug). **#4** added tests (select-all-N loop-fetch, Pager page-change, jump-to-first). **#6** memoized the pick Set. **#5 accepted** (pick mode double-fetches: the loop-fetch is required by the default `all` mode; the browser's extra paged fetch is trivial). tsc 0; vitest 69.

FE: `EntitySort` += links/evidence, 2 dropdown options at the TOP ("Most appearances"/"Most evidence"), i18n ├Ч4. **VERIFY:** go build+vet 0, api package green (incl. `TestListEntities_SortByAppearance` тАФ live PG: 3>1>0 order + trigger-maintained `cached=3`), `TestEntityOrderBy` links/evidence; FE tsc 0 + vitest 78. (The `-p1` `TestSchemaToken` blip is the same pre-existing env flake тАФ passes isolated + api package green alone.)

> Building the glossary-list overhaul plan ([`docs/plans/2026-06-14-glossary-list-overhaul.md`](../plans/2026-06-14-glossary-list-overhaul.md)) now that the RAID-block is cleared (C20/C23 forbid glossary-service edits; no remaining cycle touches it тАФ backend fully unblocked). **M1 = backend (glossary-service only, additive + backward-compatible):**
> - **Migration** `UpGlossarySearch` ([`internal/migrate/entity_search.go`](../../services/glossary-service/internal/migrate/entity_search.go)) тАФ `pg_trgm` + GIN trigram indexes on `cached_name`, `glossary_aliases_text(cached_aliases)` (IMMUTABLE wrapper so the aliases expr can be indexed тАФ PG18 treats bare `array_to_string` as non-immutable), and `attribute_translations.value`. Wired in main.go after UpEntityRevisions.
> - **Sort whitelist** (`entityOrderBy`, [`entity_search.go`](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/entity_search.go)) тАФ `name`/`name_desc`/`kind`/`status`/`alive`/`created_at[_asc]`/`updated_at[_asc]` mapped to fixed ORDER BY (no interpolation); raw mode defaults to **relevance** (exact-first, then trigram sim). Sort-by-`cached_name` (indexed). **Counts-sort DEFERRED** per DESIGN REVIEW ┬з3.5 (correlated subqueries тЖТ expensive at 20K; needs denorm counters).
> - **Raw search** (`listEntities` `search_mode=raw`) тАФ entity-side mirror of the chapter raw-search: ILIKE-exact PRIMARY (CJK-safe) + `pg_trgm` ranking over `cached_name`/`cached_aliases` (+ display-language translated-name leg), 256-rune cap. Each hit carries a `match` payload `{field_code, snippet, highlights}` with **rune-offset** highlights (pure `buildEntityMatch`/`computeEntityHighlight`, ported from `book-service/search.go`). Simple mode unchanged (no `match`).
> - **Migration-deadlock fix** (`execGuarded`, migrate.go) тАФ wrapped the DDL migrations in a transaction-scoped advisory lock (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`). Adding the new migration to the shared test harness exposed a **pre-existing** cross-package `migrate.Up` race (parallel `go test ./...` packages migrating the shared dev DB тЖТ 40P01). The lock serializes concurrent migration DDL (uncontended/instant at prod startup). **Residual:** migration-DDL-vs-test-DML contention across packages is pre-existing parallel-suite flakiness (the unrelated `grantclient BaseURL` flake also surfaces under parallel runs) тЖТ **run the glossary integration suite per-package or with `-p 1`**.

Pure helpers: 6 unit tests (escape/rune-fold/highlight/field-preference/sort-whitelist incl. injection). **Diff surgical** (entity_handler.go hunks all in my regions; no stray gofmt churn).

(2) **FE** тАФ `defaultModelsApi` + `DefaultModelsCard` (rerank picker, optimistic+rollback) mounted top of SettingsтЖТProvidersTab. (3) **knowledge consume** тАФ `reranker_client.get_default_rerank` + retriever rerank gate now resolves project model **else user default** (12 lines; also feeds the future glossary raw-search). **Storage/routes are generic (rerank+embedding)** but the **embedding picker is intentionally NOT exposed** (/review-impl MED-1: a query-time embedding default would break retrieval тАФ must match the index-time model; index-time consume is a follow-up). **VERIFY:** provider-registry go build+vet+test; knowledge 38 tests (retriever fallback+not_configured, raw_search, reranker_client incl get_default_rerank); FE tsc 0 + DefaultModelsCard 2/2; **live smoke (cross-service)** тАФ rebuilt provider-registry+knowledge тЖТ via gateway GET default-models=`{}`, set+get+clear round-trip with a real rerank-capable model, PUT bogusтЖТ400, unknown-capтЖТ400. **/review-impl: MED-1 (embedding no-op picker) FIXED** (removed; BE stays generic), LOW-1 (get_default_rerank test) FIXED, LOW-2 (deactivated default shows as orphan) accept+doc. **Deferred `D-DEFAULT-EMBEDDING-CONSUME`** тАФ wire embedding default at project-create/index time (RAID-hot knowledge build flow).

(2) **PLAN-ONLY** (user: "cс║г FE/BE nh╞░ng chс╗Й l├кn plan") to scale the glossary list to 20K+ entities: [`docs/plans/2026-06-14-glossary-list-overhaul.md`](../plans/2026-06-14-glossary-list-overhaul.md). Plan covers server-side pagination, multi-column sort, **raw lexical search mirroring the chapter raw-search** (`book-service/search.go`: ILIKE-exact-primary + pg_trgm ranking + rune-offset highlights тЖТ adapt to entity name/aliases/translations, add `search_mode=raw` + per-row `match` payload + GIN trigram migration), and a shared `<EntityListBrowser>`.
